markdown:
Interlocutors are the most talented surgeon-sculptors of the velstracs, carving away flesh and replacing it with new body parts of muscle, sinew, and metal. Each interlocutor structures their individual appearance carefully, but all are towering, multi-limbed amalgamations of the strongest limbs, densest bone, and sharpest metal they can find. They continually search for new material to graft to their forms, and their slain foes are rarely found intact, as little is more valuable to interlocutors than a powerful opponent's legs, eyes, or even brain. Interlocutors average 9 feet tall and weigh approximately 800 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Interlocutor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=851) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 285, [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 84 **Perception** +24; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), painsight **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +20 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 **Painsight** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) conditions, as well as the value of those conditions. --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +21 **Will** +26 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 215 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by good or silver) **Immunities** cold **Weaknesses** good 15, silver 15 **Glimpse of Stolen Flesh** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it sees pieces of its own body amid the interlocutor's form. The creature must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or become [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** claw +25 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+13 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 33 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) - **4th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×2), [Restoration](/Spells.aspx?ID=258) (×2) - **5th** [Breath of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=29) - **7th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (self only; to the Material Plane or [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) only) **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The interlocutor stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must immediately attempt a Will save against Glimpse of Stolen Flesh. In addition, if the creature was already [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), on a failed save, it feels its internal organs twist and writhe, and is [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 minute. After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune until the start of the interlocutor's next turn. **Surgical Rend** This functions as the [Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30) ability, dealing claw damage. In addition, if the target is a living creature with organs and muscle, the interlocutor opens a precise wound. Until the creature is restored to its maximum Hit Points, thus closing the wound, Strikes against the creature deal 1d6 extra precision damage.
name: Interlocutor

Eremites roam the planes to seek out ideal portions of other creatures, such as a hero's sword-arm or an angel's pinions. Eremites capture these specimens to clinically test their true limits, then harvest specimens and add them to their own bodies. An eremite might attach tongues to their hand as extra fingers or a fist to the back of their neck in a horrid “improvement.” Eremites average 7 feet tall and weigh approximately 200 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Eremite](/Monsters.aspx?ID=852) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 285 **Perception** +34; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), painsight, [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +40, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +36, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +34, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +36, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +36 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +10 **Painsight** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) conditions, as well as the value of those conditions. --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +37 **Ref** +32 **Will** +34 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 375 , regeneration 25 (deactivated by good or silver) **Immunities** cold, [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), nonlethal **Weaknesses** good 20, silver 20 **Ignore Pain** An eremite's actions can't be disrupted due to damage or Strikes (such as [Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)).
**Paralytic Perfection** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it feels compelled to offer pieces of its own flesh to the eremite. The creature must succeed at a DC 40 Will save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** jaws +39 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d8+19 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and exquisite pain **Melee** claw +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d6+19 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), exquisite pain, and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 42 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) - **7th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Dimensional Lock](/Spells.aspx?ID=71), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (to Material Plane or [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) only), [Shadow Siphon](/Spells.aspx?ID=274) (at will), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) - **9th** [Bind Soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=21), [Blade Barrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=24), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×2), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×2), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273), [Shadow Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=275) (at will) - **Constant (9th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Rituals** DC 42 - **8th** [Imprisonment](/Rituals.aspx?ID=13) **Evisceration** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) **Requirements** The eremite has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The eremite excises flesh or bone from a creature it has grabbed. The target takes 6d10 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). **Exquisite Pain** An eremite's knowledge of pressure points and pain centers is unsurpassed. A creature hit by an eremite's melee Strikes must succeed at a DC 40 Fortitude save or be [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (stunned 4 on a critical failure). A creature that critically succeeds is temporarily immune for 24 hours. **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The eremite stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must immediately attempt a Will save against Paralytic Perfection. In addition, if the creature was already [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), on a failed save, its unnatural longing causes it to become [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9). After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune until the start of the eremite's next turn. **Graft Flesh** **Requirement** The eremite holds a piece of flesh they collected via Evisceration; **Effect** The eremite attaches the stolen flesh to themself. They either regain 100 Hit Points; reduce the value of their [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) condition by 3; or reduce the stage of any affliction affecting them by 3. **Shadow Traveler** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47)) When an eremite uses [_plane shift_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) or [_shadow walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=275), they arrive at exactly their intended destination.
name: Eremite

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Violet Fungus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=853)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

At a glance, a violet fungus might seem to be little more than an unsightly and sickeningly purple mushroom of unusual size. Only once one draws closer— once whip-like tentacles dripping with flesh-rotting venom slither out from the fungus's cratered cap—does the terrifying truth of this carnivorous toadstool become apparent. Many amateur spelunkers have met untimely ends in the clutches of this monstrous fungus's tentacles, since violet fungi are practically synonymous with caverns on Golarion.

 Anyone who has ever braved the world's caves for an extended period of time know the dangers of the deadly violet fungus. Darklands dwellers such as drow and duergars often bear the long, whip-like scars of at least one brush with this vicious plant's cruel, poison-infused tentacles. Canny subterranean trappers and scouts sometimes make use of cultivated violent fungi to catch game in the enormous caverns below the surface world. Some Darklands peoples also cultivate violet fungi as a means to defend their territory. [Xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102) in particular place violet fungi around the perimeters of their settlements as a first line of defense.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fungus](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/VioletFungus.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Violet Fungus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=853)</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Fungus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=77" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=108" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 286

**Perception** +8; no vision, [tremorsense 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +0

**Con** +3

**Int** -5

**Wis** +1

**Cha** -2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +6 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Weaknesses**
fire 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 1d10+4 bludgeoning plus violet rot

**Violet Rot**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison plus [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison plus enfeebled 1 and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison plus enfeebled 1 and drained 1 (1 round)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Deadly Whips</title>

Canny adventurers can harvest a defeated violet fungus's tentacles and use them as [whips](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48). The whips inflict violent rot (with the same effects and DC as in the violet fungus's stat block) on anything they touch, including the wielder, although metal gloves such as gauntlets can protect against the poison. Such whips can be used for 1 hour before they degrade and become useless.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Violet Venom</title>

Alchemists and poisoners cherish violet fungi for the plants' potent rotting poison, and have perfected an effective method of preserving the toxin as [violet venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=651). After slaying a violet fungus, a character can extract the fungus's toxin with a successful DC 20 [Nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=22) or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) check. Afterward, the extracted venom can be [Crafted](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) into 1 dose of violet venom with a successful DC 18 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check.
</aside>
name: Violet Fungus

markdown:
A voracious, flesh-eating carnivore, the viper vine has a single enormous blossom arising from a thick, leafy tangle of snake-like vines. When the plant senses the approach of suitable prey through its sensitive, shallowly buried root system, it rises up like an agitated snake and unfurls its brightly colored bloom, an act that releases a cloud of mind-numbing pollen. Persistent stories claim the viper vine can lure prey into its clutches by swaying with a hypnotic motion, but this effect is in fact created by this invisible, odorless pollen cloud. Since a viper vine gains nourishment by consuming creatures rather than through ingesting moisture and soil, it has developed rudimentary locomotion and can drag itself along the ground with its tentacle-like roots. It even has a form of rudimentary sentience, allowing it to not only discern differences in prey and make limited tactical decisions, but also to avoid creatures that are particularly large or dangerous-looking. The area around viper vine hunting grounds is often strewn with the partially devoured remains of victims. It's not unusual to find the rotting corpses of wild animals, ill-fated adventurers, and even giants in the plant's immediate vicinity, along with a scattering of incidental treasure left behind on the corpses. A viper vine rarely returns to the carcass of a creature it killed earlier, preferring to hunt fresh meat. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Viper Vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=854) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 287 **Perception** +22; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [tremorsense (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** -4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -3 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +24 **Will** +22 **HP** 270 **Resistances** poison 15 **Cold Vulnerability** When exposed to a [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) effect, the viper vine is overwhelmed by lethargy, becoming [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1d4 rounds. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** jaws +27 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+11 piercing plus 3d6 poison **Melee** vine +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+11 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Captivating Pollen** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) The viper vine releases a 60-foot emanation of invisible pollen that stays in the air for 5 rounds unless dispersed by a moderate or stronger wind. Each creature that enters or starts its turn in the area must attempt a DC 33 Will save or be captivated. The viper vine can't use Captivating Pollen for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Captivating Pollen for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), and it must spend each of its actions to move closer to the viper vine as expediently as possible while avoiding obvious dangers. If a captivated creature is adjacent to the viper vine, it stays still and doesn't act. It ceases to be fascinated if it's no longer in the pollen aura at the end of its turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, plus the creature is [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 24 hours. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 3d8+8 bludgeoning, DC 33
Viper Vine Pollen While viper vine pollen degrades quickly after it is harvested carefully from the plant, a character who has a set of alchemical tools can gather and preserve 1d6 doses of pollen with a successful DC 33 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) check and 10 minutes of work. A single dose of viper vine pollen is worth 300 gp as raw materials for crafting any alchemical or magical item that creates an [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) effect.
name: Viper Vine

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Void Zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=855)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A void zombie arises when a humanoid dies from an [akata's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=541) void death affliction. This walking corpse is animated by a larval akata attached to the deceased creature's brain, using a grotesque feeding tendril that emerges from the corpse's mouth to drink blood from its victims.

 A void zombie typically exists for only a few months before it collapses and the larval akata crawls free from the motionless shell. When it feels the natural end of its unlife approaching, a void zombie finds a secluded place to vomit forth the nearly mature akata larva, then withers away. The disgorged larva metamorphizes into a full-grown akata several hours later, usually eating the corpse as its first meal.

 The name “void zombie” is something of a misnomer; though still compelled by necromantic energies, a void zombie is a host in the life cycle of a parasitic alien, not a mindless, reanimated corpse (despite their similar appearances). The fact that a typical void zombie shares the neutral alignment of the larval akata within, rather than the intrinsically evil nature of other zombies, has caused some scholars to push to reclassify them under other names. Suggestions have included “void dead,” “akata spawn,” or “bloodwalker,” but the visceral and compelling commonplace name has proven difficult to shed.

 The driving force behind a void zombie's violence rises from the ravenous hunger of the alien parasite that serves as the channel for its animating negative energies. Necromancers have tried often to duplicate the void zombie, but without a larval akata hosted within, the result is simply a zombie with missing jaws or, at best, one that moves faster than normal. Others have compared a captive void zombie to a captive [yellow musk thrall](/Monsters.aspx?ID=868) in an attempt to discover a potential link between the two— after all, both are animated bodies controlled by a parasitic outside force. Strangely, a void zombie becomes particularly violent when encountering a yellow musk thrall, making direct comparisons of the two creatures particularly difficult, or even dangerous. The fact that a void zombie, unlike a yellow musk thrall, is actually undead may hold some clues as to the source of this strange behavior.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/VoidZombie.png" />

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Void Zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=855)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 288

**Perception** +3; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** -2

**Con** +2

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 13 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +3 

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 26
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)

**Weaknesses**
salt water 5, slashing 2

**Salt Water Vulnerability** Salt water acts as an extremely strong acid on the larval [akata](/Monsters.aspx?ID=541) inside the void zombie. Full immersion in salt water deals 4d6 acid damage per round. On any round in which the void zombie takes damage due to its salt water weakness, the larval akata retreats to the depths of the void zombie's body, causing the void zombie to become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) until the end of its next turn.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +8,
**Damage** 1d6+3 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
feeding tendril +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 piercing plus Feed on Blood

**Feed on Blood** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The void zombie's previous action was a successful feeding tendril Strike against the target creature; **Effect** The void zombie uses its feeding tendril to drain blood from the creature struck. The void zombie regains 4 Hit Points, and the creature is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) until it receives healing of any kind or amount.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Non-Evil Undead</title>

Void zombies are unusual in that their animating negative force is provided by a living parasite that survives within their corpses, controlling their nervous systems for defense and to hunt food. As such, the soul of a person who succumbs to an akata's void death is not bound to its rotting corpse at all and travels on to judgment in the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) unimpeded. Nevertheless, most worshippers of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) and crusaders against undeath still consider void zombies to be anathema and fight to destroy them.
</aside>
name: Void Zombie

markdown:
Legendary creatures lurking in remote lakes, water orms always find their way into the tavern tales of lakeside communities. To some travelers, every lake of respectable size seems to be surrounded by towns full of fishers claiming to have spotted a water orm. These elusive creatures inhabit lakes mainly in cool and gloomy regions. Some claim that water orms are an offshoot of [sea serpents](/Monsters.aspx?ID=361) and [linnorms](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=69), but no credible link between these creatures has been found. Water orms have many features that sea serpents do not, such as the ability to understand the rudiments of language. Their natural inclination to avoid contact and remain hidden often remains at odds with their equally compelling curiosity about those they might spy upon the shores of their lakes. Water orm sightings usually occur when they can't help but to rise up to the surface to take a peek at someone (or something) particularly unusual on the beach or floating on the water's surface. These creatures are extremely long-lived and can go for decades, or even centuries, with very little to eat. This allows water orms to subsist in lakes without surfacing for many years, even in bodies of fresh water without ample food sources. Water orms might lie in a silty lake bed for years, their elusiveness only contributing to their mythical reputation. When a pet or child goes missing near a lake, rumors might hold that the local water orm is responsible, leading to folk tales that caution residents against venturing out alone near the water. While most water orms are described as serpentine or long-necked reptiles, others look similar to bizarrely elongated seals or whales, impossibly large sea horses, or long-necked creatures with paddles resembling those of [elasmosauruses](/Monsters.aspx?ID=645). **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Water Orm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=856) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 289 **Perception** +21; darkvision **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** -3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 **Slow Metabolism** A water orm can go for 10 years without feeding. Beyond this limit, the water orm's hunger causes it to become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), but doesn't otherwise impact its lifespan. A water orm that's slowed as a result of starvation can remove this condition by using Swallow Whole to gulp down a meal.
**Undetectable** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A water orm automatically tries to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=43), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=138), or [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=141) [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) attempted against it, using its [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) modifier for the counteract check. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +19 **Will** +17 **HP** 170 **Resistances** cold 10, fire 10 --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 50 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tail +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Large, 2d8+8 bludgeoning, Rupture 22 **Water Travel** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) A water orm can dissolve into water, appearing only as a long, dark, serpentine stretch of water. While in this form, a water orm's swim Speed increases to 600 feet, it automatically succeeds at [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39), and it gains a +4 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks in water. A water orm can remain in this form for 8 hours, but it can't enter salt water when using this ability. A water orm can return to its normal form using a single action which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
Local Orms People who live by the lakes inhabited by legendary water orms have a tendency to give local lake monsters names that sound somewhat homey or even adorable. As a result, such creatures are often regarded as local mascots or goodluck charms—particularly in lakeside settlements that depend on fishing as a significant income source.
name: Water Orm

Wereboars tend to be aggressive and stubborn, and often live with their own kind in small, remote communities. Even when they're inclined to interact with others, wereboars' short tempers and hostile nature lead other creatures to avoid them. Lone wereboars who settle down—often on remote farms—are extremely territorial, though others are content to roam and explore lands far from home. Their other habits also vary widely from individual to individual, right down to diet: some wereboars might feast on human flesh, while others are mostly vegetarian. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 16 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Wereboar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=857) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 290 **Perception** +8; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [scent (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); boar empathy **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +5, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 **Boar Empathy** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The wereboar can communicate with boars and pigs. **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) (2), [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +5 **Will** +8 **HP** 45 **Weaknesses** silver 5 **[Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dagger +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d4+6 piercing **Melee** tusk +10, **Damage** 1d8+6 piercing plus curse of the wereboar **Melee** claw +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d4+6 piercing **Ranged** dagger +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing **Boar Charge** The wereboar Strides twice and then makes a melee Strike. As long as they moved at least 20 feet, they gain a +2 circumstance bonus to their attack roll. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The werecreature changes into their humanoid, hybrid, or animal shape. Each shape has a specific, persistent appearance. A true werecreature's natural form is their hybrid shape. In humanoid shape, the werecreature uses their original humanoid size, loses their jaws and claw Strikes, and gains a melee fist Strike that deals bludgeoning damage equal to the slashing damage dealt by their claw. In animal shape, their Speed and size change to that of the animal, they gain any special Strike effects of the animal that they didn't already have (such as [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)), and they lose their weapon Strikes.
Human with fist +10 for 1d4+2 bludgeoning, or [boar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=52) with Speed 40 feet and tusk for 2d6+4 piercing. **Curse of the Wereboar** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) This curse affects only [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91); **Saving Throw** DC 15 Fortitude. On each full moon, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or turn into a wearboar until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling unconscious until dawn. **Moon Frenzy** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a full moon appears in the night sky, the werecreature must enter hybrid form, can't Change Shape thereafter, becomes one size larger, increases their reach by 5 feet, and increases the damage of their jaws Strike by 2. When the moon sets or the sun rises, the werecreature returns to humanoid form and is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) for 2d4 hours.
name: Wereboar

markdown:
These ferocious werecreatures stalk prey with the cunning and skill of a true apex predator. Weretigers typically view all life as a potential meal. Powerful nocturnal hunters with excellent senses that help them ambush prey, weretigers are adaptable to an extreme range of environments. However, weretigers living in densely populated cities (potentially as courtesans, assassins, or guild leaders) often struggle to suppress their killer instincts, becoming overwhelmed by the urge to hunt. They prefer to wear loose-fitting clothing and rely on their claws and jaws in a fight. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Weretiger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=858) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 291 **Perception** +11; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [scent (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); tiger empathy **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +7, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -1 **Tiger Empathy** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The weretiger can communicate with felines. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +13 **Will** +9 **HP** 75 **Weaknesses** silver 5 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** jaws +14, **Damage** 2d6+7 piercing plus curse of the weretiger and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d4+7 slashing **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The werecreature changes into their humanoid, hybrid, or animal shape. Each shape has a specific, persistent appearance. A true werecreature's natural form is their hybrid shape. In humanoid shape, the werecreature uses their original humanoid size, loses their jaws and claw Strikes, and gains a melee fist Strike that deals bludgeoning damage equal to the slashing damage dealt by their claw. In animal shape, their Speed and size change to that of the animal, they gain any special Strike effects of the animal that they didn't already have (such as [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)), and they lose their weapon Strikes.
Human with fist +14 for 1d4+7 bludgeoning, or [tiger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=69) with Speed 30 feet and Wrestle. **Curse of the Weretiger** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) This curse affects only [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91); **Saving Throw** DC 18 Fortitude. On each full moon, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or turn into the same kind of werecreature until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling unconscious until dawn. **Moon Frenzy** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a full moon appears in the night sky, the werecreature must enter hybrid form, can't Change Shape thereafter, becomes one size larger, increases their reach by 5 feet, and increases the damage of their jaws Strike by 2. When the moon sets or the sun rises, the werecreature returns to humanoid form and is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) for 2d4 hours. **Pounce** The weretiger Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the weretiger began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), they remain hidden until after this ability's Strike. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** claw
name: Weretiger

markdown:
Jealous guardians of tombs, barrows, and sepulchers, cairn wights usually spawn from necromantic rituals. For those mortals who cannot abide the thought of separation from their earthly possessions, the undead existence offered by transformation into a cairn wight can be tempting. Perhaps as frequently, particularly avaricious and wealthy royalty or merchants seek out victims to transform into cairn wights to guard their precious wealth for all time.
Only in the rarest instances is the greed of a mortal strong enough to spontaneously transform them into a cairn wight without a dark ritual or the intercession of a powerful divine being. On those occasions, however, the resultant wight exhibits unmatched viciousness and likely owns rare treasure indeed.
As guardians of material possessions, cairn wights are supernaturally bound to the armaments they wore during the ritual used to create them. They can spread their necromantic powers into the weapons they wield. A slash from a cairn wight's sword channels life from the victim into the wight. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Cairn Wight](/Monsters.aspx?ID=859) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 292 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31), [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5) --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +10 **Will** +11 **HP** 67 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Final Spite** **Trigger** The cairn wight is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The cairn wight makes a Strike before being destroyed. It doesn't gain any temporary HP from drain life on this Strike. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** longsword +14 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d8+7 slashing plus drain life **Melee** claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d6+7 slashing plus drain life **Cairn Wight Spawn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A living humanoid slain by a cairn wight's weapon or claw Strike rises as a spawned wight after 1d4 rounds. This spawned wight is under the command of the cairn wight that killed it. It doesn't have drain life or cairn wight spawn and is [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for as long as it is a spawned wight. If its creator dies, the spawned wight becomes a full-fledged, autonomous cairn wight; it regains its free will, gains drain life and cairn wight spawn, and is no longer clumsy. **Drain Life** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the cairn wight damages a living creature with a melee Strike, using an unarmed attack or its bound weapon, the cairn wight gains 5 temporary Hit Points and the creature must succeed at a DC 18 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). Further damage dealt by the cairn wraith increases the drained condition value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4. **Funereal Dirge** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The cairn wight chants a low, haunting melody. Living creatures within 50 feet must attempt a DC 21 Will save. The cairn wight can't chant a new Funereal Dirge for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 2 and takes a –2 status penalty to saving throws against drain life.
Cairn Wight Dirges Unlike a typical wight, cairn wights have an unusual affinity for music. The lyrics of their dirges often contain a valuable bit of knowledge or long-lost lore, possibly the greatest treasure a cairn wight guards. There are even brave souls who seek out the creatures to listen to their songs in hopes of finding profit from the dry, rasping words.
name: Cairn Wight

markdown:
Manifesting as a sinuous form wreathed in sickly green flames, this incorporeal undead forms when a powerful [hag](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=59) or [witch](/Classes.aspx?ID=16) dies in agony or rage. The body at the center of the flames usually resembles the witch's idealized self-image, regardless of their age and appearance at the time of their death. A witchfire often resides in a bog or swamp, which slowly blackens and decays around them. These regions are often plagued by [will-o'-wisps](/Monsters.aspx?ID=414), which seem to have an uncanny relationship with the resident witchfire. A witchfire might visit villages on the edge of their home terrain to lure away villagers, but always attack victims within earshot to make sure that other villagers hear the screams. A witchfire might also ally with other hags or witches and even join their covens, but a witchfire is incapable of forming covens entirely on their own. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 28 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Witchfire](/Monsters.aspx?ID=860) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 293 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19 **Str** -5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +0 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 **[Coven](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=11)** The witchfire adds [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=208), [_phantasmal killer_](/Spells.aspx?ID=219), [_phantasmal calamity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=218), and [_summon entity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=321) to their coven's spells. A coven can contain one or more witchfires, but it must also include at least one living creature capable of forming a coven; three witchfires cannot form a coven. --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +21 **Will** +18 **HP** 125 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), fire, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all 10 (except force, [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or positive; double resistance vs. non-magical) **Opportune Witchflame** **Trigger** A creature makes a melee Strike against or touches the witchfire; **Effect** The witchfire makes a witchflame caress Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** witchflame caress +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d6 fire plus 3d6 negative and witchflame **Ranged** witchflame bolt +21 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6 fire plus 2d6 negative and witchflame **Occult Innate Spells** DC 28 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132) - **2nd** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) (at will) - **3rd** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) - **4th** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) - **5th** [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57) - **6th** [Summon Entity](/Spells.aspx?ID=321) (will-o'-wisp only) **Witchflame** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Any creature that takes negative damage from a witchfire's Strike must attempt a DC 26 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to witchflame for 1 hour.
**Success** The creature appears to catch fire with sickly green flames. These flames deal no damage on their own, but as long as the flames burn, the creature can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) ([invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) creatures are concealed rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39)) and gains weakness 5 to fire for 1 round.
**Failure** As success, but the effect is permanent until removed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature gains weakness 10 to fire. **Witchflame Kindling** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirements** A creature within 30 feet of the witchfire burns with witchflame; **Effect** The witchfire's eyes and mouth glow brightly, and the witchflame on the target creature momentarily burns much hotter, dealing 5d6 fire damage and 5d6 negative damage to that creature (DC 28 basic Fortitude save). The witchfire can't use Witchflame Kindling for 1d4 rounds.
Wisps and Fire Witchfires have the ability to summon [will-o'-wisps](/Monsters.aspx?ID=414), but a number of these aberrations usually linger nearby of their own accord. The fear-inducing witchflame aura manifested by witchfires provides will-o'-wisps their favored sustenance.
name: Witchfire

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Witchwyrd](/Monsters.aspx?ID=861)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Witchwyrds conceal most of their faces, leaving only their eyes unmasked. Their inscrutability is a boon to one of their most significant interests—mercantilism— and many haughty witchwyrds openly revel in the befuddlement inspired by their mysterious guises and mannerisms. Witchwyrds have a keen eye for new opportunities and markets, and a witchwyrd almost always knows when someone is trying to pull one over on them.

 These four-armed humanoid creatures have hairless blue-gray skin, are typically 6-1/2 feet tall, and weigh 300 pounds. Their hands have three evenly sized and spaced digits in a tripod-like arrangement. When not trying to blend in with the local community, witchwyrds favor outlandish, loose-fitting clothes in bright reds or yellows and a signature conical hat. They tend to prefer the driest, warmest regions of the lands they visit—perhaps an indicator of their mysterious home world. This, however, is the only indicator; witchwyrds are notoriously close-mouthed about details of this distant world, and with good reason: most witchwyrds on Golarion have never visited their ancestral home. To these witchwyrds, the notion of a home planet is a constant thorn in their side, and when asked, many choose to ignore the question altogether. Others are so agitated by these queries that they respond with impatience or even violence. Some scholars have theorized a connection between witchwyrds and several other four-armed creatures, but like with questions of their place of origin, witchwyrds have little to say about the topic.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Witchwyrd.png" />
</column>

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +4

_+1 [ranseur](/Weapons.aspx?ID=35)_

---

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 22 

**Fort** +13 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 110

</row>

**Resistances**
force 5

**Absorb Force** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** A [_magic missile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=180) is fired at the witchwyrd, and the witchwyrd is aware of it and has a free hand; **Effect** The witchwyrd “catches” the missile, absorbing it and causing that hand to glow while it holds this energy. A hand that's holding energy can't be used for any other purpose except to use Force Bolt. The energy lasts for 6 rounds or until it is released.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_ranseur_ +16 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 1d10+6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 23
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66)
- **1st**
[Floating Disk](/Spells.aspx?ID=124) (at will), [Unseen Servant](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) (at will)
- **2nd**
[Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197)
- **3rd**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78)
- **4th**
[Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) (×2), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315), [Resilient Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=255)
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Force Bolt** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) The witchwyrd fires one [_magic missile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=180) per action spent (dealing 1d4+1 force damage each). They can't spend more actions on this ability than they have free hands. If they use a hand that has Absorbed Force, that hand hurls two missiles instead of one, expending the held energy.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Alien Allies</title>

While witchwyrds often appear to be solitary creatures, the most successful among them rarely wander the planes and planets alone. High-ranking or wealthy witchwyrds employ a variety of bodyguards to accompany them, and their entourage is usually composed of a variety of strange beings met during their travels.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Wolverine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=862)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wolverines resemble small bears, to which they are related. Stocky and muscular with long claws, wolverines are about 3 feet in length and weigh approximately 40 pounds. They have a thick fur coat that protects them from the cold temperatures of the forests and frozen tundras where they reside.

**Wolverine Rage** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The wolverine enters a state of pure rage that lasts either for 1 minute, until there are no enemies it can perceive, or until it falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), whichever comes first. This worm that walks was a cultist of the Great Old One [Hastur](/Deities.aspx?ID=162) in its prior life. It continues its conspiracies and strives to further its evil plots in its new life. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Worm That Walks Cultist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=864) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 297 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +27, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28 **Str** +5 **Dex** +8 **Con** +5 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +8 --- **AC** 36 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1) **Fort** +23 **Ref** +28 **Will** +26 **HP** 200 ([fast healing 10](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15)) **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=36), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 10 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **Discorporate** When the worm that walks is reduced to 0 HP, it discorporates and the component worms that make up its body disperse in every direction. If even a single worm escapes, the worm that walks will eventually re-form using a process that typically takes 1d10 days.
While the exact circumstances and surroundings determine how long the worm that walks's foes have to dispatch the fleeing worms before they escape, usually its foes have only a single round. Typically, this requires the application of an area effect or [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) weapon within 1 round to the space where the worm that walks collapsed. After any amount of area or splash damage is dealt to that space, the character dealing the damage must attempt a DC 15 flat check. Each subsequent area or splash damage effect performed on the area reduces the DC of this flat check by 2, to a minimum of DC 5. If any of these flat checks succeed, none of the worms escape, and the worm that walks is destroyed permanently. At the GM's discretion, clever means of trapping or otherwise detaining the vermin may extend the time allowed to finish off the worm that walks. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** tendril +29 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+14 [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 34, attack +26 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=195), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Comprehend Language](/Spells.aspx?ID=46), [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=158), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [Undetectable Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=348) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=90), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=156), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) (4 slots) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Modify Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=200), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315), [Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=355) (4 slots) - **5th** [Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=193), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=314) (4 slots) - **6th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214), [Summon Entity](/Spells.aspx?ID=321) (4 slots) - **7th** [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219), [Visions of Danger](/Spells.aspx?ID=358), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) (3 slots) **Squirming Embrace** The worm that walks Strides, ending its movement sharing a space with a creature, and deals 5d8 piercing damage to the creature. The creature can attempt a DC 32 basic Reflex save. **Swarm Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The worm that walks collapses into a shapeless swarm of worms. It drops all held, worn, and carried items. While discorporated, the worm that walks can't use [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15) actions and can't cast spells, but it can move through areas small enough for its individual worms to fit without having to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31). It can use the same action to coalesce back into its normal form.
name: Worm That Walks Cultist

markdown:
These menacing spiritual remnants of wicked warlords or bloodthirsty generals are towering specters of shadow and death. Like other wraiths, dread wraiths haunt the shadowy places of the world, but dread wraiths are more likely to travel greater distances to sow terror or amass influence, often sticking to dark glades or sinister ruins when journeying across sun-dappled lands. Dread wraiths tends to be arrogant and rarely form a pack with others of their kind, preferring instead to dominate groups of ordinary wraiths. A particularly powerful necromancer might compel packs of dread wraiths into service, however, while a particularly malevolent goal—such as to eradicate a bastion of light and life—might draw several dread wraiths together in a common purpose. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Dread Wraith](/Monsters.aspx?ID=865) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 298 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19 **Str** -5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +19 **Will** +21 +1 status to all saves vs. positive **HP** 130 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all 10 (except force, [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or positive; double resistance vs. non-magical) **Draining Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) 10 feet. A creature entering the aura must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). It recovers after it has been out of the aura for 1 minute. This drained condition value is cumulative with the drained value caused by drain life, but not with multiple draining presence auras. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to draining presence for 24 hours.
**Sunlight Powerlessness** A dread wraith caught in sunlight is [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3).
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** fly 60 feet **Melee** spectral hand +21 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+7 negative plus drain life **Absorb Wraith** The dread wraith extends its hand toward another [wraith](/Traits.aspx?ID=260) creature within 100 feet. The target wraith dissolves and streaks toward the dread wraith in a straight line, dealing 6d10 negative damage to each creature along the line (DC 28 basic Fortitude save). The dread wraith absorbs the essence of the target wraith, becoming [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) and gaining a +10-foot status bonus to its fly Speed for a number of rounds equal to the level of the absorbed wraith. It can use its extra action only to Fly or Strike. An unwilling target can attempt a DC 28 Will save to resist being absorbed. **Drain Life** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the dread wraith damages a living creature with its spectral hand Strike, the wraith gains 10 temporary Hit Points and the target must succeed at a DC 28 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). Further damage dealt by the wraith increases the drained condition value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4. **Wraith Spawn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A living [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) slain by a wraith's spectral hand Strike rises as a wraith spawn after 1d4 rounds. This wraith spawn is under the command of the dread wraith that killed it. It doesn't have drain life or wraith spawn. If the creator of the wraith spawn dies, the wraith spawn becomes a full-fledged, autonomous wraith; it regains its free will and gains drain life and wraith spawn.
Dread Wraith Origins The most unusual dread wraiths are those that coalesce from an amalgamation of evil spirits, often in regions where such spirits are shredded from their consciousnesses and churned in foci of negative energy, such as the [Negative Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=2) or on the Isle of Terror.
name: Dread Wraith

Xills visit the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), where they're often known as ethereal stalkers, primarily to maraud and to kidnap creatures back to their native [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9). These four-armed, warmongering monsters paralyze their victims before implanting them with eggs using an ovipositor normally kept retracted behind their mandibles. Their single-minded drive to expand and conquer is so all-encompassing that while they work together dutifully and without dissent, there is little else in their lives. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 24 **[Recall Knowledge - Ethereal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Xill](/Monsters.aspx?ID=866) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 299 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Items** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), _+1 [longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=76)_ (40 arrows), [Scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=38) (2), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10) --- **AC** 24 (26 with shield raised) **Fort** +15 **Ref** +17 **Will** +11 **HP** 100 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** scimitar +16 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 2d6+7 slashing **Melee** bite +16, **Damage** 2d8+7 piercing plus xill paralysis **Ranged** _longbow_ +17 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)), **Damage** 2d8 piercing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 24 - **7th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (to [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9) or [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only; self only) **Double Slash** ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) The xill makes two scimitar Strikes against a single target. If both hit, combine their damage for the purpose of the target's resistances and weaknesses. The xill applies its multiple attack penalty to each strike normally. **Implant** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The xill is adjacent to a [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), willing, or [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) creature; **Effect** The xill implants xill eggs in the creature's flesh. **Xill Eggs** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) The [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition from xill eggs doesn't improve on its own until the disease is cured or runs its course. It can be cured with a 10-minute operation that requires a successful DC 26 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check and deals 4d6 slashing damage to the host; **Saving Throw** DC 24 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 24 hours; **Stage 1** infested with no ill effect (8 hours); **Stage 2** sickened 1 (8 hours); **Stage 3** sickened 2 (4 hours); **Stage 4** sickened 2 and 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) as larval xills burrow out of the body and immediately fade away into the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9) (1 hour) **Xill Paralysis** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A creature hit by the xill's bite Strike must attempt a DC 24 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is immune to xill paralysis for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is paralyzed. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to recover, and the save DC decreases by 1 each round.
The Ether War Xills see [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) creatures as little more than incubators, but the need for fresh hosts never abates, for on their home plane, xills are constantly at war. Only the relative emptiness of the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9) keeps their conflict with creatures like [ether spiders](/Monsters.aspx?ID=203) from becoming too overwhelming and outpacing the speed at which new ranks of soldiers can replace those who have fallen.
name: Xill

Made up of thick, green vines with numerous yellow flowers, the yellow musk creeper grows at places where the ground is enriched with spilled blood and corpses, like battlefields and graveyards. An ideal environment for the yellow musk creeper isn't inhabited, but draws occasional humanoid visitors the plant can transform into thralls. The yellow musk creeper enraptures creatures with its bright yellow pollen to bring them close. It then physically alters their brains with its tendrils, implanting a seed in each to turn the creatures into mindless thralls. The yellow musk creeper might try to take over a sleeping creature, but it lacks the faculties to sneak up on anyone, and is likely to wake victims before it can drag itself close enough. The plant's pollen can spread far on the wind but quickly becomes too diffuse to cause harm. Wilderness scouts learn to sniff out the distinctive musky scent of the pollen, which stays potent even in harmless quantities. A sprouting creeper is also easy to detect, since it grows from the rotting, messy corpse of a thrall creature. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Yellow Musk Creeper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=867) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 300 **Perception** +4; [tremorsense (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) **Languages** **Skills** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6 **Str** +3 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +6 **Will** +4 **HP** 34 **Immunities** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) **Weaknesses** fire 5 --- **Speed** 5 feet **Melee** tendril +9 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d10+3 piercing **Bore into Brain** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The creeper bores dozens of tendrils into the brain of a Small, Medium, or Large [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature that's [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), willing, or [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by Spray Pollen, and within reach of the creeper's tendrils. The creature must succeed at a DC 18 Fortitude save or become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (stupefied 2 on a critical failure). Subsequent failed saves against Bore into Brain increase the stupefied value. If the creature reaches stupefied 5, it is turned into a [yellow musk thrall](/Monsters.aspx?ID=868). Boring into a creature's brain doesn't end fascination caused by Spray Pollen. **Spray Pollen** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) The yellow musk creeper blasts yellow pollen in either a 30-foot line or a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the emanation must attempt a DC 20 Will save. Once a creature succeeds at any save against Spray Pollen, it becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14). For as long as it is fascinated, it must spend each of its actions to move closer to the yellow musk creeper as expediently as possible, while avoiding obvious dangers. If the creature is adjacent to the yellow musk creeper, it stays still and doesn't act. If anyone takes a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the creature or its allies, the effect ends. Otherwise, the creature can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns. On a success, the effects end.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the condition doesn't end automatically if anyone takes a hostile action against the creature or its allies.
Massive Musk Creepers Ancient or specially cultivated musk creepers can grow to great size. Their pollen often changes color as they grow, and pollen of different colors can alter the creeper's thralls in different ways. Some creepers even become intelligent after they're awakened by primal magic. These few exert far more control over their thralls.
name: Yellow Musk Creeper

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Yellow Musk Thrall](/Monsters.aspx?ID=868)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A creature transformed by a yellow musk creeper's tendrils boring into its brain becomes a yellow musk thrall if it's Small or Medium, or a yellow musk brute if Large (a Small creature becomes a Small thrall rather than a Medium one). The transformation takes 1 hour. A yellow musk thrall takes only the most rudimentary of actions: bringing the yellow musk creeper water and new creatures to infest. After a few days (or if the creeper dies), the thrall wanders off into the wilderness and falls dead within 24 hours. The body of a deceased thrall sprouts a new yellow musk creeper within hours of its death, becoming a fully grown plant within 24 hours.

**Limb Extension** <actions string="Free Action" />  **Trigger** The yellow musk thrall is reduced to 4 HP or fewer; **Effect** Creeper tendrils tear through the thrall's limbs, causing its forearms to tear loose. The thrall's melee reach increases by 5 feet.

**Pollen Touch**   When the thrall strikes a creature, that creature is exposed to yellow musk creeper pollen, as [Spray Pollen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=867) (DC 14). The creature is fascinated by the yellow musk creeper that spawned the thrall, not by the thrall itself.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Yellow Musk Brute](/Monsters.aspx?ID=869)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A creature transformed by a yellow musk creeper's tendrils boring into its brain becomes a yellow musk thrall if it's Small or Medium, or a yellow musk brute if Large (a Small creature becomes a Small thrall rather than a Medium one). The transformation takes 1 hour. A yellow musk thrall takes only the most rudimentary of actions: bringing the yellow musk creeper water and new creatures to infest. After a few days (or if the creeper dies), the thrall wanders off into the wilderness and falls dead within 24 hours. The body of a deceased thrall sprouts a new yellow musk creeper within hours of its death, becoming a fully grown plant within 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

**Slow** A yellow musk brute is permanently [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) and can't use reactions.

</column>

---

**Entangling Tendrils** ([aura Entangling Tendrils](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 5 feet. Grasping tendrils extend out from the brute. When the brute ends its movement next to a creature, or a creature ends its turn next to the brute, that creature must succeed at a DC 15 Reflex save or be [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the tendrils.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +11 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),

**Limb Extension** <actions string="Free Action" />  **Trigger** The yellow musk brute is reduced to 15 HP or fewer; **Effect** Creeper tendrils tear through the brute's limbs, causing its forearms to tear loose. The brute's melee reach increases by 5 feet.

**Pollen Touch**   When the brute strikes a creature, that creature is exposed to yellow musk creeper pollen, as [Spray Pollen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=867) (DC 15). The creature is fascinated by the yellow musk creeper that spawned the brute, not by the brute itself.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Recovery from Thralldom</title>

A creature that's transformed into a yellow musk thrall or brute doesn't die right away, although it no longer retains its personality, memories, or abilities as long as it exists as a thrall. Within the first few days after its transformation, a thrall or brute can be restored to its normal life with any magical effect that can remove disease or a 1-hour procedure that requires a successful DC 22 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) (expert) check and deals 2d6 slashing damage to the thrall or brute.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-162" />
name: Yellow Musk Brute

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Yeth Hound](/Monsters.aspx?ID=870)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wicked canine creatures who live for the thrill of the hunt, yeth hounds often serve evil masters as guardians and trackers. Yeth hounds resemble lean, sleek dogs with overlarge ears and narrow paws that can tread on air as easily as the ground. Despite their canine appearance, yeth hounds are remarkably intelligent, although they rarely display this intelligence except when devising intricate tactics to ensnare their quarry. Their eerie bays echo across the countryside when they are engaged in a hunt, and they particularly enjoy baying to frighten and disorient intelligent creatures. Yeth hound packs can number as many as a dozen members, each working in uncanny communion with its packmates to corner and kill their prey. Yeth hounds like to drag their victims back to their lairs to eat at their leisure, so these lairs often contain discarded treasures from the hounds' previous meals.

 Yeth hounds despise two things: sunlight and other canines. They hunt only at night if they can, often breaking off their hunt at dawn to retreat to a subterranean lair or den, no matter how close they had come to catching their prey. Yeth hounds usually attack [wargs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=98), [wolves](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=101), and similar creatures on sight, working to drive larger or more powerful canines from their hunting areas when then can't simply overpower and kill them.

 Evil rangers, bestial [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), and wicked cults are frequently gifted yeth hound servants by fiendish patrons. Such hounds often serve as spies and are quick to turn against those who fail to advance the patrons' wicked aims. The cult of [Lamashtu](/Deities.aspx?ID=11), in particular, is fond of using yeth hounds as temple guardians.

<column gap="tiny">

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18

**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +8

</row>

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +11,
**Damage** 1d8+6 piercing plus 1d6 evil, [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20), and sinister bite

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 20
- **Constant (4th)**
[Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6)

**Bay** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The yeth hound emits an unearthly howl audible up to 300 feet away. Any non-[fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) creature that hears the howl must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). Any creature that critically fails and is within 60 feet of the yeth hound is instead frightened 3 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) for 1d4 rounds (or until it recovers from its frightened condition). Whether it succeeds or fails the saving throw, the creature is then temporarily immune to Bay for 24 hours.

**Sinister Bite**   A good creature bitten by a yeth hound must attempt a DC 20 Will save. On a critical success, the creature is temporarily immune to sinister bite for 1 minute. A creature that fails becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), or increases the value of its frightened condition by 1 if already frightened.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Yeth Hound Allies</title>

When an evil cult engages the services of a yeth hound, the process is more like forging an alliance than animal training. Yeth hound allies can provide much more complex services than a typical animal, but they also present the risk of possible treachery.
</aside>
name: Yeth Hound
