Mighty plant-dragons, zomoks prowl the woodlands, defending their homes from destructive forces while healing and restoring the woods following natural disasters and ravaging intruders. Zomoks vary in appearance with the seasons, growing verdant green in the spring and summer, changing to autumnal colors in the fall, and decaying to brown in winter. While zomoks can and often do swallow their foes, they have no real need to eat. Any creature they gulp down is typically left behind as a mangled corpse the next time the zomok travels via their Forest Step ability. Zomoks reach 35 to 40 feet in length and weigh 30 tons. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 35 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Zomok](/Monsters.aspx?ID=871) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 303 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +29, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +30 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +9 **Int** +3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +25 **Will** +28 **HP** 310 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145), [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) **Weaknesses** fire 15 --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 80 feet; [_pass without trace_](/Spells.aspx?ID=215) **Melee** jaws +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+17 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing **Melee** tail +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 34, attack +26 - **1st** [Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215) - **6th** [Tangling Creepers](/Spells.aspx?ID=331) - **7th** [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=366) - **8th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (plant creatures only) - **Constant (4th)** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Rituals** DC 34 - **4th** [Blight](/Rituals.aspx?ID=4), [Plant Growth](/Rituals.aspx?ID=18) - **6th** [Primal Call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=19) (doesn't require secondary casters) **Entombing Breath** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The zomok blasts forth a 60-foot cone of earth and gravel, dealing 17d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 34 basic Reflex save); [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to this saving throw. Creatures touching the ground that fail their saves take a 10-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds and are [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 34) on a critical failure. The zomok can't use Entombing Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
The bodies of creatures slain by Entombing Breath instantly decompose and are destroyed—they can be restored to life only by a 7th-level or higher spell or ritual that restores life. The creature's equipment is unharmed but buried under 5 feet of earth. **Forest Step** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The zomok teleports itself with the effects of a 5th-level [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) spell, but the zomok must begin and end the teleportation effect in forest terrain. The zomok regains 10d10 Hit Points during this teleportation. Creatures swallowed by the zomok are left behind. The zomok can't use Forest Step again for 1d6+1 rounds. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Huge, 3d12+9 bludgeoning, Rupture 35 **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Huge or smaller, tail, DC 32
Zomoks' Primal Call Zomoks can use [_primal call_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=19) without needing any secondary casters. The zomok typically calls an [awakened tree](/Monsters.aspx?ID=28), [arboreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8), or [shambler](/Monsters.aspx?ID=364), and often tasks the called creature with guarding a sacred location, accompanying an ally on a mission for the good of the forest, or a similar assignment.
name: Zomok

<title level="1" pfs="">[Ammut](/Monsters.aspx?ID=872)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These chimeric, desert-dwelling fiends have massive bodies that are part hippopotamus, part large cat, and part crocodile. Though they devour souls for sustenance, ammuts are infamously picky eaters; most will only eat experienced adventurers or, for a special treat, particularly evil creatures, passing over common folk or those whose mettle has never been tested. 

Only a few ammuts are known to exist, and most dwell in Osirion. However, recent expeditions into Orv's Vault of the Black Desert have uncovered the existence of ammuts in this region, as well.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 43

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Ammut.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 18">[Ammut](/Monsters.aspx?ID=872)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 77

**Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +33, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +29, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +7

**Con** +9

**Int** +3

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +7
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 41 

**Fort** +35 

**Ref** +27 

**Will** +28 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 350

</row>

**Immunities**
evil

**Resistances**
fire 20, poison 20

**Weaknesses**
good 20

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 50 feet, burrow 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+16 slashing plus wasting wound

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
foot +30 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174)),
**Damage** 4d8+12 plus [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (DC 38 Fortitude negates)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +32 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d12+16 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 37
- **6th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87)
- **Constant (6th)**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (evil only), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The ammut exhales a blast of flame that deals 18d6 fire damage to all creatures in a 30- foot cone (DC 37 basic Reflex save). The ammut can't use their Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

**Devour Soul**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A creature whose HP drops to 0 while swallowed by an ammut has its soul consumed, as well as its body. The creature dies instantly and cannot be brought back to life except by [_miracle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=196), [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377), or similar magic.

**Fast Swallow** <actions string="Reaction" />  **Trigger** The ammut [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) a creature; **Effect** The ammut uses Swallow Whole.

**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** <actions string="Single Action" />  Large, 3d12+8 bludgeoning, Rupture 32

**Wasting Wound**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A creature dealt damage by an ammut's claw Strike must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or become [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (or enfeebled 2 and drained 2 on a critical failed save). The conditions cannot be mitigated until the curse is removed, and the curse cannot be removed until all damage dealt by the claw Strike is healed. Each day, a creature afflicted with wasting wound must attempt another saving throw; on a failed save, the values of its enfeebled and drained conditions each increase by 1. If the victim dies while cursed, its soul is destroyed, as per the ammut's devour soul ability.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Ammut Diet</title>

In an article published in volume 36 of the _Pathfinder Chronicles_, Tarana Geshepalamori recounts surviving an ammut attack after the beast dismissed her as “a bland soul.” She subsequently interviewed the fiend, who shared that an ammut “tastes” souls in much the same way a chef tastes the elements of a dish; the most delectable souls are those that have faced (or, especially, inflicted) great suffering and evil.
</aside>
name: Ammut

Whereas typical [djinn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=212) can cleanse or clear areas with their arcane winds, the air magic of muurfeli is tainted by the Black Desert's noxious radiation and mixed with the realm's ambient fetid winds. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Muurfeli](/Monsters.aspx?ID=873) **Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 78 **Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) **Languages** [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31 **Str** +7 **Dex** +9 **Con** +5 **Int** +5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=38)_ --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +31 **Will** +27 **HP** 300 **Resistances** fire 15, poison 15 **Weaknesses** cold 10 **Firedamp Winds** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 30 feet, DC 33. Swirling winds of noxious, flammable gas surround a muurfeli. An open flame, spark, or similar fire effect in the area causes an explosion that deals 20d6 fire damage to all creatures in the aura (including the muurfeli) and temporarily disables the aura for 1 minute. When the muurfeli takes damage from an ability with the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait or fails a saving throw against a water effect, their firedamp winds aura is disabled for 1 minute. A creature that enters or begins its turn in the aura must attempt a Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The creature is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2 and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15). --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** fist +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+15 bludgeoning plus 2d6 poison **Melee** _scimitar_ +31 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 3d6+17 slashing plus 2d6 poison **Ranged** fetid winds +31 ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d8+7 bludgeoning plus 2d8+5 poison **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 36 - **2nd** [Enhance Victuals](/Spells.aspx?ID=101) (at will) - **4th** [Burning Hands](/Spells.aspx?ID=30) (at will), [Gaseous Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=129), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only) - **5th** [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=158) - **7th** [Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42) - **Constant (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The muurfeli can take on the form of an owl. The owl form always looks the same, but the muurfeli can choose to become either Tiny or Small in this form. This transformation doesn't change their Speed or the attack and damage bonuses of their Strikes, but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to slashing).
name: Muurfeli

[Shaitans](/Monsters.aspx?ID=213) corrupted by the Black Desert's blightburn become scorpion-like arardas. While other earth genies sparkle with the iridescence of gemstones and evoke the power of the earth itself, arardas use their connection to the ground to harness the might of electricity. Arardas are so infused with this radioactive crystal that their mere presence is toxic to most living creatures. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Ararda](/Monsters.aspx?ID=874) **Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 79 **Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [tremorsense (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) **Languages** [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Black Desert Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +27, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +33, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +33 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +3 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [maul](/Weapons.aspx?ID=33)_ --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +30 **Will** +31 **HP** 280 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) **Resistances** electricity 20 **Weaknesses** sonic 20 **Blightburn Radiation** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) 60 feet. Blightburn crystals infused into an ararda's body attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) spell moving into or out of the aura's emanation (counteract check +31; 7th level). In addition, any creature that starts its turn within the emanation is exposed to [blightburn sickness](/Diseases.aspx?ID=21). The aura is blocked by lead sheeting, 1 foot of stone, or large force effects (such as a [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=363), but not a [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) spell). An ararda can suppress their aura with an action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
**Charged Earth** ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56)) Any creature that hits an ararda with an unarmed attack or touches the ararda takes 2d8 electricity damage. Weapons that hit an ararda also take 2d8 electricity damage. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 45 feet, fly 20 feet; earth glide **Melee** _maul_ +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 3d12+17 bludgeoning **Melee** sand fist +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+17 bludgeoning plus 2d8 electricity and [blightburn sickness](/Diseases.aspx?ID=21) **Ranged** sand spear +32 ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d8 piercing plus 2d8 electricity and [blightburn sickness](/Diseases.aspx?ID=21) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 37 - **4th** [Gaseous Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=129) (self only), [Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136) (×3) - **5th** [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276) (at will) - **6th** [Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=355) (self only), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=365) - **7th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33), [Flesh to Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=123) - **Constant (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The ararda can take on the form of an scorpion. The scorpion form always looks the same, but the ararda can choose to become either Tiny or Small in this form. This transformation doesn't change their Speed or the attack and damage bonuses of their Strikes, but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to piercing). **Earth Glide** The ararda can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through dirt and stone at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.
name: Ararda

markdown:
The art of golem animation is not limited to societies with access to expensive crafting materials or a long history of arcane mastery. Among the resource-poor [xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102) who dwell in the Darklands, especially those hardy clutches that call the Black Desert their home, obsidian is one of the most common crafting materials available. Combine this ingredient with a powerful spellcaster and the xulgath tradition of inflection—magically warping and working normally inflexible stone—and the end result is an obsidian golem. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Obsidian Golem](/Monsters.aspx?ID=875) **Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 80 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +24 **Will** +26 **HP** 230 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), fire (see below), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), magic (see below), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), nonlethal attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 15 (except adamantine or bludgeoning) **Assimilate Lava** An obsidian golem that submerges itself in lava or magma takes no damage from it, and for as long as it remains submerged, it becomes [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) and gains [fast healing 15](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15).
**Golem Antimagic** harmed by sonic (8d10, 2d10 from area or persistent damage); healed by cold (area 2d10 HP); [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) by fire
**Vulnerable to Shatter** Casting a [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) spell on an obsidian golem affects the golem normally, but also causes jagged chunks of obsidian to flake off its body. The squares on which the golem was standing (or the squares below it, if it was flying) become difficult terrain. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** fist +33 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 4d10+15 piercing **Melee** wing +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d10+15 piercing **Ranged** obsidian sliver +32 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 4d8+15 piercing **Fling Shards** The obsidian golem slings razor-sharp shards of itself in a 15-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 4d8+9 slashing damage (DC 37 basic Reflex save). **Shred Armor** An obsidian golem's body is so sharp that it tears through armor like a knife through butter. Whenever the golem scores a critical hit with a fist Strike against a creature wearing non-[adamantine armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=139), the creature's armor becomes [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2).
Glass Avatars [Demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28) worshippers in particular show a strong propensity toward creating obsidian golems, often designing these constructs in the image of their patron demon lord.
name: Obsidian Golem

markdown:
The slithering horror known as Iffdahsil is an undead [shoggoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=368) that has haunted the vault of the Black Desert for millennia. [Drow](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=37) legends in particular tend to feature Iffdahsil, terrifying the elves from a young age. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 52 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 52 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 50 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 [Iffdahsil](/Monsters.aspx?ID=876) **Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 81 **Perception** +38; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21), [scent (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33), [tremorsense (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +40, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +36 **Str** +11 **Dex** +9 **Con** +10 **Int** +0 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 43 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1) **Fort** +39 **Ref** +36 **Will** +35 +1 status to all saves vs. magical effects **HP** 380 ([fast healing 30](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15), [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), critical hits, [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyze](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** acid 20, cold 20, physical 15 (except magical silver), sonic 20 **Weaknesses** good 20 **Aura of Annihilation** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 60 feet, DC 45. A creature entering or starting its turn in the aura that fails at a Fortitude save becomes [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). If the target was already doomed or drained, the values of these conditions increase by 1, to a maximum of doomed 3 and drained 3. A creature that dies while doomed 3 and drained 3 in this way disintegrates into black sand.
**Unkillable** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Unlike most undead, Iffdahsil isn't destroyed at 0 HP. Iffdahsil's fast healing continues to function normally, though on any round it begins its turn at 0 HP, it is [enfeebled 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) until the start of its next turn. While Iffdahsil is at 0 HP, it can be killed permanently only by simultaneously exposing it to direct sunlight (or a 10th-level [_sunburst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=326) spell) and casting a [_miracle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=196) or [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) spell (or similar magic) directed at destroying it. --- **Speed** 40 feet, burrow 40 feet, climb 25 feet **Melee** pseudopod +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 5d10+21 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 42 - **7th** [Hallucinatory Terrain](/Spells.aspx?ID=145) - **8th** [Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=73) (at will; self only), [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144) - **9th** [Overwhelming Presence](/Spells.aspx?ID=212) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 3d10+17 piercing **Eat Away** A creature that begins its turn inside Iffdahsil takes 10d6 negative damage. Iffdahsil gains temporary HP for 1 minute equal to half the damage dealt. **[Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14)** DC 43, 10d6 negative, Escape DC 43, Rupture 45 **Tentacular Burst** Iffdahsil deals 8d8 piercing damage to each creature in a 10-foot emanation (DC 43 basic Reflex save) and can also attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) any creatures who critically fail their save as a free action. Iffdahsil is then [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of its next turn.
Mysterious Origins Iffdahsil's path to undeath is unknown. Many believe Iffdahsil can consume and incorporate the souls and minds of its victims, which might explain its spellcasting powers and relatively advanced intellect, though this also gives rise to more unanswerable questions about its horrific nature and unknowable goals.
name: Iffdahsil
resistance_markdown: acid 20, cold 20, physical 15 (except magical silver), sonic 20

An urdefhan cult's best tormentors can advance to become truly formidable daemon summoners. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Urdefhan High Tormentor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=877) **Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 82 **Perception** +22; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +18, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +22 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +3 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=47)_ --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +16 **Will** +22 **HP** 195 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects (except necrotic decay), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Weaknesses** positive 10 **Necrotic Decay** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) When an urdefhan dies, its invisible flesh quickly rots away and sublimates into a foul-smelling gas that fills a 5-foot emanation around the body. This gas deals 10d6 negative damage to creatures in this area as their flesh curdles and rots as well (DC 29 basic Fortitude save). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _warhammer_ +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 2d8+11 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d6+11 piercing plus wicked bite **Divine Innate Spells** DC 30, attack +22 - **1st** [Feather Fall](/Spells.aspx?ID=111) (self only), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) (×2) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63), [False Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=108), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=287) - **3rd** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×2), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) (×2) - **4th** [Acid Arrow](/Spells.aspx?ID=2), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219), [Spiritual Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=306) - **5th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=19), [Summon Fiend](/Spells.aspx?ID=323) (daemons only) **Divine Rituals** DC 30 - **1st** [Daemonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=25) **Stoke the Fervent** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The urdefhan lets out a battle cry, sending itself and its allies into a fanatical frenzy. Each ally that hears the call gains a +4 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and a +2 status bonus to saving throws, and takes a -2 status penalty to AC. Affected allies must use at least one of their actions to Strike each round, if they are able (even if it means attacking an ally, object, or thin air). This lasts for 2d4 rounds. **Wicked Bite** **Requirements** The urdefhan damaged a creature with a jaws Strike on its last action; **Effect** The urdefhan maintains contact, turning the creature's flesh translucent around the site of the injury. The urdefhan chooses one of two options, each of which requires a DC 29 Fortitude save. If the jaws Strike was a critical hit, the creature suffers both effects, using the same save result for both.
  • **Drain Blood** The urdefhan drinks some of the creature's blood. On a failed save, the creature is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and the urdefhan regains 5 HP (or, on a critical failure, it's drained 2 and the urdefhan regains 10 HP).
  • **Drain Vitality** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The urdefhan draws out some of the creature's vital essence. The creature becomes [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) for 1 hour on a failed save (or enfeebled 2 for 1 hour on a critical failure).
name: Urdefhan High Tormentor

Urdefhan hunters serve as their cults' trappers, hunters, and poachers. Many urdefhan hunters use [skavelings](/Monsters.aspx?ID=803) to traverse the massive caverns of the Darklands and scout for prey. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Urdefhan Hunter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=878) **Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 82 **Perception** +26; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +25 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +1 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74)_ with 50 arrows, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [rhoka sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=120)_ --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +26 **Will** +23 **HP** 190 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects (except necrotic decay), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Weaknesses** positive 12 **Necrotic Decay** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) When an urdefhan dies, its invisible flesh quickly rots away and sublimates into a foul-smelling gas that fills a 5-foot emanation around the body. This gas deals 12d6 negative damage to creatures in this area as their flesh curdles and rots as well (DC 32 basic Fortitude save). --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _rhoka sword_ +24 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing **Melee** jaws +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d6+10 piercing plus wicked bite **Ranged** _composite longbow_ +26 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)), **Damage** 2d8+4 piercing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +23 - **3rd** [Feather Fall](/Spells.aspx?ID=111) (at will; self only), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) - **4th** [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63) **Deadly Aim** ([Open](/Traits.aspx?ID=123)) The urdefhan hunter makes a ranged Strike at a –2 penalty. The urdefhan deals 4 additional damage with this Strike. **Double Shot** **Requirement** The urdefhan hunter is wielding a ranged weapon with [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254); **Effect** The urdefhan hunter makes two Strikes with the required weapon at a single target. These Strikes both count toward the urdefhan's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until both attacks are resolved. **Mounted Bow Expert** Urdefhan hunters can make composite longbow Strikes while mounted. **Wicked Bite** **Requirements** The urdefhan damaged a creature with a jaws Strike on its last action; **Effect** The urdefhan maintains contact, turning the creature's flesh translucent around the site of the injury. The urdefhan chooses one of two options, each of which requires a DC 32 Fortitude save. If the jaws Strike was a critical hit, the creature suffers both effects, using the same save result for both.
  • **Drain Blood** The urdefhan drinks some of the creature's blood. On a failed save, the creature is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and the urdefhan regains 5 HP (or, on a critical failure, it's drained 2 and the urdefhan regains 10 HP).
  • **Drain Vitality** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The urdefhan draws out some of the creature's vital essence. The creature becomes [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) for 1 hour on a failed save (or enfeebled 2 for 1 hour on a critical failure).

This gas deals 15d6 negative damage to creatures in this area as their flesh curdles and rots as well (DC 34 basic Fortitude save). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _rhoka sword_ +26 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing **Melee** jaws +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 4d6+10 piercing plus wicked bite **Divine Innate Spells** DC 35, attack +29 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Forbidding Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=126), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Feather Fall](/Spells.aspx?ID=111) (self only), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63), [False Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=108), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=287) - **3rd** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×2), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) (×2) - **4th** [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) (×2), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247), [Spiritual Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=306) - **5th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=19), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Prying Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=239) - **6th** [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76), [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) (×2) - **7th** [Dimensional Lock](/Spells.aspx?ID=71) **Eyes of the Enthralled** **Requirement** The urdefhan dominator has a creature dominated with its [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) innate spell; **Effect** The urdefhan taps into the dominated creature's sight, hearing, and any other senses it has. markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[Vitalia](/Monsters.aspx?ID=880)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Foul occult magic, rare alchemical reagents, and copious amounts of spilled blood are the keys to creating a vitalia—also known as a heart horror— though thankfully the exact means of their creation has been long forgotten.

 Now, most vitalias now come into being by sheer accident. This unusual life-form cannot live without regular contact with blood, much like how fish cannot live long outside of water. Because of this, vitalias are rarely found outside the lairs of mad alchemists or wizards, living in pools of the life-giving substance. Physically, a vitalia somewhat resembles a blood-red [giant jellyfish](/Monsters.aspx?ID=707), though it also bears some semblance to a giant human heart.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 43

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Vitalia.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 18">[Vitalia](/Monsters.aspx?ID=880)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=168" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=108" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 84

**Perception** +30; bloodsense (precise) 30 feet, [tremorsense (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40), no vision

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +5

**Con** +9

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Bloodsense** The vitalia can detect any creatures that have a heartbeat.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 39 

**Fort** +35 

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +24 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 425
([fast healing 20](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15))

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)

**Weaknesses**
piercing 15

**Blood Eruption** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature lands a critical hit with a melee Strike against the vitalia **Effect** The vitalia sprays blood at the triggering creature. The creature must succeed at a DC 39 Reflex save or become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round (or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round on a critical failure).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, swim 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
vein +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d8+15 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
blood squirt +33 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 3d10 bludgeoning plus 3d10 acid

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 36
- **6th**
[Status](/Spells.aspx?ID=308) (at will), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354)
- **8th**
[Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=353)

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** <actions string="Single Action" />  4d8+10 bludgeoning, DC 37

**Forced Transfusion** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The vitalia is grabbing a creature; **Effect** The vitalia inserts some of its veins into the grabbed creature and pumps violently, rapidly exchanging the victim's blood with its own. Any [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) or [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) affecting the vitalia can be transmitted to the target creature, which must make a saving throw as though it had just been targeted with the original affliction. The inverse is true as well; the vitalia must successfully save against any diseases or poisons affecting the target or contract the affliction.

**Sanguine Rain** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The vitalia pumps copious amounts of blood into the air, creating a cascading rain of vital fluids in a 15-foot emanation. All creatures in the area are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from one another. A creature that benefits from consuming blood (such as [bloodseekers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=51) or [vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97)) gains [fast healing 10](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) while in the area. While in the area, a creature taking [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) cannot end that condition, even if it spends actions to attempt a flat check to do so. The rain lasts for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Living Livers</title>

A vitalia's ability to filter and purify even days-old blood makes them highly sought after by certain powerful individuals, especially frail leaders or moguls in pursuit of eternal youth, vampires, or other creatures with sanguinary predilections.
</aside>
name: Vitalia

<title level="1" pfs="">[Xulgath Deepmouth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=881)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Xulgath deepmouths wield the powers of the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) against their clutch's enemies and invoke righteous fear in their followers. The vast majority of deepmouths worship demon lords, most commonly [Zevgavizeb](/Deities.aspx?ID=82). Scalescribing—the warping of xulgath flesh into imitations of [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12) script—is a widespread practice among deepmouths, enabling them to channel the power of the Abyss through their fanatical devotees' own bodies.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 32

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Xulgath_XulgathDeepmouth.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Xulgath Deepmouth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=881)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
<trait label="Xulgath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=244" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 85

**Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +25, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +25, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +22

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +4

**Con** +2

**Int** +4

**Wis** +7

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spiked gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=11)_, [_+1 hide armor_](/Armor.aspx?ID=7), bone [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of [Zevgavizeb](/Deities.aspx?ID=82), [Material Component Pouch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=33)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +25 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 215

</row>

**Stench** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) 30 feet. A creature that enters the area must attempt a DC 28 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and on a critical failure, the creature also takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. While within the aura, the creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saves to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to all xulgaths' stenches for 1 minute.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_spiked gauntlet_ +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Free-Hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=181), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d4+6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +20,
**Damage** 2d6+6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 2d4+6 slashing

**Divine Prepared Spells** DC 33, attack +27
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)
- **1st**
[Protection](/Spells.aspx?ID=238), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) (×2)
- **2nd**
[Status](/Spells.aspx?ID=308) (×3)
- **3rd**
[Heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=149), [Meld into Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=188), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354)
- **4th**
[Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) (×2)
- **5th**
[Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×2), [Ravening Maw](/Spells.aspx?ID=647)
- **6th**
[Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×6), [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=254) (×2), [Enervation](/Spells.aspx?ID=687)

**Cleric Domain Spells** DC 33, 3 Focus Points
- **6th**
[Commanding Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=408), [Vibrant Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=467)

**Divine Rituals** 

- **2nd**
[Consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8)
- **6th**
[Commune](/Rituals.aspx?ID=6)

**Devourer's Dictum** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The deepmouth warps part of a scalescribed creature's flesh into a mouthed tentacle, then wills it to lash out. The deepmouth makes a jaws Strike that originates from any living scalescribed creature within 120 feet; this Strike also has [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) and deals an additional 2d8 negative damage. If the Strike originates from a creature other than the deepmouth, that creature triggers reactions as though it had made the Strike.

**Scalescribe**  ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) With 10 minutes of work, a deepmouth can carve a magical rune called a scalescript on the flesh of a living, willing xulgath (including the deepmouth itself). A creature cannot be inscribed with more than one scalescribe rune at a time, and a deepmouth can create and maintain no more than three scalescripts at a time. If the deepmouth Scalescribed itself, it can provide material and somatic components while Casting a Spell without a free hand.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Noble](/NPCs.aspx?ID=882)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Envied by many and detested by still others, nobles exude confidence and gentility. Gossip and gambling are often nobles' favorite pastimes. Day-to-day life for a noble is often a mixture of business and leisure, and to an onlooker, such a lifestyle can appear to be nothing more than a string of meals, parties, and game halls. However, despite what some might believe, life as a noble is often quite dangerous and requires far more intellect than it would appear—along with a healthy dose of suspicion.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

**Lip Reader** After years of sticking their nose where it doesn't belong, the noble has learned to read lips from afar, with the effects of the [Read Lips](/Feats.aspx?ID=833) feat.

**Items**

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Palace Guard](/NPCs.aspx?ID=883)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Every palace has its gatekeepers. Often the younger offspring of minor nobility or those from long lines of trusted staff, the palace guards are in charge of defending the royal family and their inner stronghold. Their days consist of guarding doorways, escorting nobles to various locations, and keeping those in their charge as safe as possible. The statistics below are for the lowest rank-and-file member of a small nation's palace guards; they also work well for elite guards of a lesser noble.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Advisor](/NPCs.aspx?ID=884)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Trusted counselors of the court, advisors whisper words of guidance in the ears of those in power. Many nobles lean so heavily on their counsel that they make few decisions without them and insist on their attendance at all meetings and public events. Advisors are often master manipulators, and their guidance can shape a barony or even a kingdom—for good or ill.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Spy](/NPCs.aspx?ID=885)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

No empire would be complete without its spies. The spy is a master of infiltrating the court and blending in: they might be one of a number of nobles, a beloved confidante of the queen, or even the court jester. Spies use their skills to subtly manipulate courtiers, turn enemies against one another, and collect valuable information either for blackmail or to auction to the highest bidder.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Grave Robber](/NPCs.aspx?ID=886)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Many cultures have a tradition of burying their dead with a selection of the deceased's most precious possessions, and some include gifts that are thought to provide a boon in the afterlife. Such valuables can be easy pickings for those with no respect for—or fear of—the dead.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

By setting ambushes and traps in concealing countryside, such as forests and mountains, bandits waylay travelers and plunder their valuables before disappearing back to their wilderness hideouts. Many bandits seek only to steal and release their victims alive, though a few prefer to leave no witnesses.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Using their strength to bully others into submission, ruffians often work as bodyguards and enforcers for powerful criminals like charlatans and fences.

If this Strike hits, the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of the ruffian's next turn or until it leaves the ruffian's reach, whichever comes first.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The ruffian deals an extra 1d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Charlatan](/NPCs.aspx?ID=889)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Charlatans use pretense and misdirection to swindle money and other valuables from the credulous and confused. Selling snake oil (literally or figuratively), distracting marks to pick their pockets, or faking mediumship are classic grifts.

<column gap="tiny">

**Languages**

**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Cha** +4

**Versatile Performance** The charlatan can use [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) instead of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) and instead of [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53). They can also use an acting Performance instead of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46).

**Items**

Burglars specialize in breaking and entering to rob homes and businesses. These criminals focus on gaining access to secure buildings and bypassing traps and other security measures while remaining undetected all the while. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Burglar](/NPCs.aspx?ID=890) **Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 210 **Perception** +10; (11 to find traps) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +7, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +12, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +7 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Items** [Climbing Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=14), [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (10 arrows), [lesser darkvision elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=89), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Sap](/Weapons.aspx?ID=37), [Thieves' Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58) --- **AC** 21 (22 vs. traps) **Fort** +7 **Ref** +12 **Will** +10 +1 circumstance to all saves vs. traps **HP** 60 **Deny Advantage** The burglar isn't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to creatures of 4th level or lower that are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), flanking, or using surprise attack.
**Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** The burglar is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker it can see; **Effect** The burglar gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** shortsword +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d6+6 piercing **Melee** sap +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)), **Damage** 1d6+4 bludgeoning **Ranged** composite shortbow +14 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing **Mobility** When the burglar Strides half their Speed or less, that movement does not trigger reactions. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The burglar deals an extra 1d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Surprise Attack** On the first round of combat, if the burglar rolls [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) for initiative, creatures that haven't acted are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to them.

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Fence](/NPCs.aspx?ID=891)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The longer criminals hold on to stolen loot, the greater the chance they will get caught. Fences make themselves indispensable in the underworld by paying for stolen goods in order to resell them later, whether through a seemingly legitimate business or to a closed group of elite buyers.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/NPCs/Burglar_Fence.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Fence](/NPCs.aspx?ID=891)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 210

**Perception** +11

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Accounting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +13, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +10, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** +4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Fence's Eye** Fences live by their ability to recognize a viable trade. They can use [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) to identify an item's value and [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) on an item. They gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Underworld Lore checks when doing so, and to Underworld Lore checks to determine whether an item was stolen, whether a stolen item would be too recognizable to easily move, and who would be interested in purchasing such an item.

**Items**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) (10), [lesser darkvision elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=89), [Disguise Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), [_bird feather token_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=244), [lesser smokestick](/Equipment.aspx?ID=135) (2), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43), [Thieves' Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 70

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d6+5 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d4+5 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 piercing

**Quick Rummage** <actions string="Single Action" />  The fence always has a few items close at hand. The fence Interacts to draw a weapon or item that takes a single action to activate, and then Strikes with the weapon or Activates the Item.

**Scoundrel's Feint**   When the fence successfully [Feints](/Actions.aspx?ID=48), their target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the fence's attacks until the end of the fence's next turn. On a critical success, the target is flat-footed against all attacks until the end of the fence's next turn.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The fence deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-182" />
name: Fence

Assassins commit murder, either for pay or due to their belief in a cause, such as a religion or a political movement. Some assassins work alone and choose their own victims. Others are members of assassins' guilds, organizations that accept contracts to kill in return for money, favors, or both. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Assassin](/NPCs.aspx?ID=892) **Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 211 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +12, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +10, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +14, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +15, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +14 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Swift Sneak** The assassin can move their full Speed when [Sneaking](/Actions.aspx?ID=63). **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)_ (20 arrows), [lesser darkvision elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=89), [Giant Centipede Venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=113) (3), [_invisibility potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=187), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [_+1 rapier_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36), [simple injury poison](/Feats.aspx?ID=563) (8 doses, 2d4 poison damage), sleep poison (2) --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +19 **Will** +14 **HP** 130 **Deny Advantage** The assassin isn't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to creatures of 8th level or lower that are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), flanking, or using surprise attack.
**Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** The assassin is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker they can see; **Effect** The assassin gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** rapier +18 ([deadly 1d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d6+8 piercing **Ranged** composite shortbow +20 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d6+4 piercing **Poison Weapon** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The assassin is wielding a piercing or slashing weapon and has a free hand; **Effect** The assassin applies a poison to the weapon. **Quick Draw** The assassin Interacts to draw a weapon, then Strikes with that weapon. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The assassin deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Surprise Attack** On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the assassin.

Zealots ferret out plots against their religion and seek justice for their church's followers. This zealot serves [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=2), but others might serve [Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=1), [Calistria](/Deities.aspx?ID=3), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=9), [Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=13), [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14), [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=16), or [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=20). They often ride a [war horse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=268) wearing [light barding](/Rules.aspx?ID=255). This horse is an additional monster in the fight. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Zealot of Asmodeus](/NPCs.aspx?ID=895) **Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 213 **Perception** +9 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +10, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Items** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (20 arrows), [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12), [Mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=7), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10) --- **AC** 22 (24 with shield raised) **Fort** +9 **Ref** +7 **Will** +11 **HP** 58 **[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)**
**Swear Vengeance** **Trigger** A creature the zealot can see damages a follower of [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=2) other than the zealot; **Effect** The zealot gains the effect of a 1st-level [_true strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) spell. If the zealot makes an attack roll against anyone other than the triggering creature, the _true strike_ ends with no effect. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** mace +12 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 1d8+4 bludgeoning **Ranged** composite shortbow +9 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 19, attack +11 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Forbidding Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=126), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65), [Magic Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=182), [Spirit Link](/Spells.aspx?ID=302) - **2nd** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (x3), [Restoration](/Spells.aspx?ID=258), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271), [Shield Other](/Spells.aspx?ID=281) **Channel Smite** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) The zealot expends a prepared [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) spell and makes a melee Strike. Instead of the normal effect of the spell, its negative damage is added to the Strike's damage. **Deadly Simplicity** The zealot's mace deals 1d8 damage instead of 1d6.
name: Zealot of Asmodeus

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Priest](/NPCs.aspx?ID=896)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Cloistered priests safeguard their temples and communities. They are the stalwart keepers of their god's tenets, devoted to spreading the word. Their guidance or healing services come at the cost of a donation.

<column gap="tiny">

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Beggar](/NPCs.aspx?ID=897)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wherever there is poverty, there are beggars. Beggars are truly the downtrodden of society—folks who have been cast out into the streets due to a variety of setbacks or the weight of circumstance. Some beggars work for underworld organizations as lookouts, spies, or even hired muscle on the cheap—sometimes by choice, but often not.

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Commoner](/NPCs.aspx?ID=898)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Though not every commoner is downtrodden, many live hard lives of toil as they work to keep their families fed and housed in relative comfort. While many commoners are just simple folks who keep to themselves, sometimes commoners rebel, either rising up to face their oppressors or persuaded or even duped into joining nefarious plans and criminal conspiracies. Commoners benefit from power in numbers, and a crowd of commoners can accomplish a great deal more than their rulers often expect.

<column gap="tiny">

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 13

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Although their escapades might be notorious, few urchins are thrill-seekers. For some urchins, when begging alone is not enough to fill their bellies, theft becomes a viable survival tactic. Many underworld powers also use urchins as spies and messengers, while training them to become future pickpockets, burglars, and foot soldiers.

**Pickpocket**   The urchin can [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) or [Palm an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=67) that's closely guarded without taking the usual -5 penalty. They can Steal objects that would be extremely noticeable or time-consuming to remove (like worn shoes, armor, or actively wielded objects).

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Prisoner](/NPCs.aspx?ID=900)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Most who end up in a jail, dungeon, or prison are just biding their time until their imprisonment ends, trying to find ways to make it through interminable days of boredom and deprivation. Some, however, may use their time on the inside to strengthen their criminal connections. Using force and intimidation to gain status among other prisoners, they create makeshift weapons to take down their enemies and give them an edge if escape becomes possible. Even for prisoners who don't become involved in the world of prison politics, learning to stand up for themselves and projecting an air of toughness can become essential for survival in a place where people have little left to lose.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 15

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Chronicler](/NPCs.aspx?ID=902)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Exploration means very little if no account of the details exist. Members of adventuring bands cherish chroniclers who record tales of their deeds.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/NPCs/Explorer_Chronicler.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Chronicler](/NPCs.aspx?ID=902)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 216

**Perception** +14

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Map Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +17, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +14, [Scribing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +1

**Int** +4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (20 bolts), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), journal, [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), maps, _scroll of [_acid arrow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2)_, _scroll of [heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +9 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 45

</row>

**Live to Tell the Tale** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The chronicler is [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11); **Effect** The chronicler reduces their dying condition by 1. If the chronicler is no longer dying, they remain [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) for at least 1 hour or until they regain 1 Hit Point.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
staff +7 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
crossbow +7 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Primal Prepared Spells** DC 20, attack +12
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330)
- **1st**
[Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=122), [Longstrider](/Spells.aspx?ID=175), [Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215)
- **2nd**
[Entangle](/Spells.aspx?ID=103), [Flaming Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=121)

**Scroll Mastery**   The chronicler can activate any [scroll](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=52) of a 2nd-level spell or lower as if they had the spell on their spell list, regardless of its magical tradition.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-185" />
name: Chronicler

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Guide](/NPCs.aspx?ID=903)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Guides bring travelers, tourists, and adventurers into the wondrous natural world, using their expertise to avoid deadly monsters and gruesome hazards.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/NPCs/Explorer_Guide.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Guide](/NPCs.aspx?ID=903)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 217

**Perception** +14

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Scouting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** +1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (40 arrows), [Greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=21), [Scale Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

**Guide's Warning** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The guide is about to roll a Perception or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) check to determine their initiative; **Effect** The guide visually or audibly warns allies, granting them a +1 circumstance bonus to their initiative rolls. Depending on how the guide warns allies, this action has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) or [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
greataxe +12 ([Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)),
**Damage** 1d12+6 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
composite shortbow +9 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 piercing

**Guiding Words** <actions string="Single Action" />  The guide points out a weakness of a creature within 30 feet. The guide and all allies that can hear or see the guide gain a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls against that creature until the beginning of the guide's next turn. The guide adds an extra 1d6 precision damage to their weapon and unarmed attacks that hit that creature during the duration.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Tips From The Guide</title>

You might allow PCs who [Follow the Expert](/Activities.aspx?ID=4) when led by a guide to continue to add their level to the skill check for some time afterward: 1 hour if the guide is an expert, 1 day for master, or 1 week for legendary. (The guide here has expert [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3), [Scouting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), and [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16).) A character can have this benefit for only one skill at a time.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-185" />
name: Guide

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Tomb Raider](/NPCs.aspx?ID=904)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Great treasure awaits those willing to explore the hazardous depths of the world's ancient tombs and forgotten dungeons. Some tomb raiders seek riches of bygone eras; others recover pieces of history thought lost to the sands of time.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Tomb Raider](/NPCs.aspx?ID=904)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 217

**Perception** +13

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Architecture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +7, [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +11, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hazard Spotter** The tomb raider automatically attempts a secret Perception check to notice a hazard when they are within 10 feet of the hazard.

**Items**
[Climbing Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=14), [Hand Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69) (20 bolts), [_+1 kukri_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=56)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 75

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, climb 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_kukri_ +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 1d6+7 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hand crossbow +15 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+3 piercing

**Trick Attack** <actions string="Single Action" />  The tomb raider musters their guile to attempt a clever attack that catches their foe unprepared. The tomb raider chooses one of their weapons. The next attack with that weapon deals an extra 2d6 precision damage. In addition, the tomb raider can Interact to draw the weapon (or to reload it if they choose the hand crossbow).

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-185" />
name: Tomb Raider
