**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

</row>

**Bravery** When the ship captain rolls a success on a Will save against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effect, they get a critical success instead. In addition, anytime they gain the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition, reduce its value by 1.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_rapier_ +17 ([deadly 1d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 1d6+10 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
main-gauche +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=190), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d4+10 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hand crossbow +14 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing

**Dual Disarm** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The captain makes two Strikes, one with their rapier and one with their main-gauche (in either order). If both Strikes hit, the ship captain can attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) the target. Their multiple attack penalty increases only after all the attacks are made.

**No Quarter!** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The captain orders their shipmates to fight without mercy. All allied creatures of equal or lower level within 20 feet of the ship captain gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls until the end of the ship captain's next turn.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Apprentice](/NPCs.aspx?ID=955)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ambitious apprentices can be found in all cities. These individuals are generally younger and seek the approval of their masters as they learn their craft. Many yearn to exemplify the artistry behind their craft, one day becoming masters themselves. Depicted below is an apprentice cartographer.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 13

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Merchant](/NPCs.aspx?ID=956)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Small settlements can typically support one or two generalist merchants, and larger cities house multiple specialists—experts in one type of product. Merchants can be found anywhere: vendors hustling in the public square, shopkeepers running small storefronts, traveling salespeople in carriages or caravans, or wealthy tycoons running entire organizations devoted to commerce. For encounters involving negotiation or mercantile skill, a merchant is a 4th-level challenge. A merchant might have an additional [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill about a specific category of item (such as jewelry or magic weapons), with a total skill bonus 2 higher than Mercantile Lore.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +12, [Mercantile Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +10, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +8, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +0

**Con** -1

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Appraising Eye** The merchant can use [Mercantile Lore](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=125) to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about items, including determining their value. They can also attempt to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) using Mercantile Lore and can do so without first knowing whether the item is magical.

**Items**
[Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (10 bolts), [Padded Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Smith](/NPCs.aspx?ID=957)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Most smaller communities have at least one smithy where locals and travelers can have horses shod or equipment repaired. Larger settlements and cities often have a variety of smiths, many specializing in blacksmithing, weapon smithing, armor smithing, or even smelting coins in a mint. The smith is a 6th-level challenge when competing with smithing or other crafting tasks.

<column gap="tiny">

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Guildmaster](/NPCs.aspx?ID=958)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In cities, artisans working in a common trade often form guilds to set standards of quality, establish common prices, engage in collective bargaining with business owners, and lobby local governments for favorable laws. The guildmaster—often a master artisan in their own right—also acts as an administrator and politician, advocating for artisans in their trade. The guildmaster is a 12th-level challenge when competing in crafting or their area of expertise (architecture in the example below).

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/NPCs/Tradesperson_Guildmaster.png" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>

<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 245

**Perception** +16

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Architecture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Bureaucracy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +19, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +1

**Int** +4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Items**
[Artisan's Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3), construction schematics, guildmaster's uniform (functions as [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7)), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [light hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=29)_, tax ledgers

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 26 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +17 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 135

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_light hammer_ +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 2d6+5 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_light hammer_ +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 2d6+5 bludgeoning

**Call to Action** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The guildmaster gives a speech to inspire themself and all guild-member allies within 60 feet, granting them a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls until the start of the guildmaster's next turn.

**Sworn Duty**   While within the guild or presiding over guild business, the guildmaster gains a +2 circumstance bonus to weapon attack rolls and deals an additional 2d6 damage on a successful weapon attack.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Saboteur](/NPCs.aspx?ID=959)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Saboteurs excel at infiltration, using it to perform destructive acts, whether they are the physical destruction of a valuable object or obstructing important political proceedings. Unlike those of spies, the motivations of saboteurs is not to steal information, but rather to sow chaos among their enemies.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Saboteur](/NPCs.aspx?ID=959)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 246

**Perception** +8; (10 to find traps) 

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +5, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +6, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +7, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +5, [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +5, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +6, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +9, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Snare Crafting** The saboteur can [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) [snares](/Equipment.aspx?Category=31) and has the supplies to make up to two [caltrop snares](/Equipment.aspx?ID=332) and up to two [hampering snares](/Equipment.aspx?ID=336).

**Items**
[Crowbar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=18), [Disguise Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), [Hand Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69) (10 bolts), [Padded Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=3), [Sap](/Weapons.aspx?ID=37), [Snare Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=51), [Thieves' Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 (20 vs. traps)

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +9 (+11 vs. traps)

**Will** +8 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 27

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sap +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)),
**Damage** 1d6+3 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hand crossbow +9 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 piercing

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The saboteur deals an extra 1d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Sabotage!</title>

Though the saboteur included here is intended to be a villain, PCs might encounter saboteurs on the side of good. These are typically rebels against tyrannical forces. Those who take great care to avoid harming civilians are chaotic good; others are chaotic neutral.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-198" />

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Mastermind](/NPCs.aspx?ID=960)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Masterminds weave long-ranged plots to see their nefarious goals come to fruition, deftly manipulating those around them, and turning enemies into friends and then pitting them against one another. When competing in a social or intellectual arena, the mastermind is a 7th-level challenge.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/NPCs/Villain_Mastermind.png" />
</column>

</row>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 246

<row gap="medium">

**Versatile Performance** The mastermind can use [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) instead of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) and instead of [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53). The mastermind can also use an acting Performance instead of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46).

**Items**

<title level="1" pfs="Limited">[Antipaladin](/NPCs.aspx?ID=961)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

There is perhaps nothing more anathematic to peace than an antipaladin. Dedicated holy warriors to the darkest and foulest of deities, antipaladins work to fulfill the vile wishes of their evil masters. The zeal with which they implement the plans of dark gods makes them truly fearsome foes for anyone allied with the forces of good.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/NPCs/Villain_Antipaladin.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Antipaladin](/NPCs.aspx?ID=961)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 247

**Perception** +10

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +12, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Items**
[lesser cheetah's elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=86), [Greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=21), [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12), [Javelin](/Weapons.aspx?ID=71) (10)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 75

</row>

**Destructive Vengeance** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Trigger** An enemy within 15 feet damages the antipaladin; **Effect** The antipaladin increases the amount of damage they take by 2d6 and deals 2d6 damage to the triggering enemy, choosing evil or negative damage. In addition, the antipaladin deals 2 extra damage of the chosen type with their Strikes against the triggering creature until the end of the antipaladin's next turn.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
greataxe +15 ([Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)),
**Damage** 1d12+10 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
javelin +12 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 1d6+10 piercing

**Champion Devotion Spells** DC 20, 1 Focus Point
- **3rd**
[Touch of Corruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=654)

**Intimidating Strike** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The antipaladin makes a melee Strike. If they hit and deal damage, the target is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), or frightened 2 on a critical hit.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Servants Of Destruction</title>

Antipaladins are the polar opposite of [paladins](/Causes.aspx?ID=1): chaotic evil warriors devoted to destruction. Much like champions of good, they must follow a code. Their code exhorts evil and prohibits selfless acts. Antipaladins usually serve [Lamashtu](/Deities.aspx?ID=11), [Rovagug](/Deities.aspx?ID=15), or demon lords. Many antipaladins used to be champions of good and seek to destroy their former faiths.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-198" />
name: Antipaladin

**Persistent Lies** Any creature deceived by the despot's [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) skill believes the deception more readily on the next day. Any attempts to later attempt a Perception check against the despot's Deception DC take a -2 circumstance penalty, as do other creatures' attempts to convince the creature otherwise, such as through [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or further Deception.

**Items**

**Damage** 1d4+4 piercing

**Divine Spontaneous Spells** DC 23, attack +13
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)
- **1st**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Sanctuary](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) (4 slots)

- **3rd**

**Sorcerer Bloodline Spells** DC 22, 1 Focus Point
- **3rd**
[Diabolic Edict](/Spells.aspx?ID=498)

**Bloodline Magic**   When the despot casts a bloodline spell, [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=104), [_flaming sphere_](/Spells.aspx?ID=121), or [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), either a target takes 1 fire damage per spell level, or the despot gains a +1 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks for 1 round.

**Dangerous Sorcery**   When the despot Casts a Spell from a spell slot, if the spell deals damage and doesn't have a duration, the despot gains a status bonus equal to the spell's level to that spell's damage.

</column>

It's the reckless scientist's job to break the rules of reality, no matter the cost. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Reckless Scientist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=963) **Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 248 **Perception** +10 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +9, [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +13 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 **Infused Items** A reckless scientist carries the following infused items: 2 [moderate acid flasks](/Equipment.aspx?ID=74), 2 [moderate alchemist's fires](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75), 1 [lesser bomber's eye elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=83), 2 [moderate frost vials](/Equipment.aspx?ID=77), and 3 [lesser elixirs of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91). These items last for 24 hours, or until the next time the scientist makes their daily preparations. **Items** [Alchemist's Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2), [moderate antidote](/Equipment.aspx?ID=80), [moderate antiplague](/Equipment.aspx?ID=81), bandolier, [_+1 sickle_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=9), work coat (functions as [padded armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=3)) --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +14 **Will** +10 +1 status to all saves vs. poison **HP** 92 **Resistances** poison 5 **Unstable Compounds** When an attacker scores a critical hit against the reckless scientist, one of the scientist's poorly stowed alchemical items bursts. The GM determines the item randomly. If it was a bomb, the alchemist takes damage from the bomb, and any creature adjacent to the alchemist takes the splash damage. Any other item is simply wasted. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _sickle_ +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d4+7 slashing **Ranged** alchemical bomb +16 ([range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150)), **Damage** varies by bomb **Quick Bomber** The reckless scientist Interacts to draw a bomb, then Strikes with it. **Reckless Alchemy** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The reckless scientist is holding a [bomb](/Traits.aspx?ID=21) or [elixir](/Traits.aspx?ID=59); **Effect** The reckless scientist combines the bomb with another bomb or the elixir with another elixir. They can Interact to draw a second bomb or elixir if necessary as part of this action. They attempt a DC 28 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check, destroying both component items to create one new item. If a viable resulting item isn't used by the end of the scientist's next turn, it explodes as described under critical failure.
**Critical Success** The new item has the full effect of both component items when used.
**Success** The new item combines both items, but halves the effect of each. (This halves damage for bombs, the amount of healing of [elixirs of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91), or the duration for effects that can't have their value halved. Details are determined by the GM.)
**Failure** The new item is inert.
**Critical Failure** The new item immediately explodes, dealing 3d6 piercing damage to the reckless scientist.
Alter Ego Mutagen A reckless scientist may have a special mutagen with a 1-round onset that transforms them into another specific NPC or monster of their level, with the same duration as other mutagens. All statistics change, though HP changes only if the new maximum HP is lower than the scientist's current HP.
name: Reckless Scientist

markdown:
Cutthroats, killers, thieves, and toughs prey upon the vulnerable of society. Directing their activities and keeping them in line are the duties of the gang leader. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Gang Leader](/NPCs.aspx?ID=964) **Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 249 **Perception** +14 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +15, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** -1 **Cha** +4 **Items** [_minor healing potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=186), [_+1 shortsword_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43), [Sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72) (10 bullets), [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5), [Tanglefoot Bag](/Equipment.aspx?ID=78) --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +17 **Will** +10 **HP** 104 **Deny Advantage** The gang leader isn't flat-footed to creatures of 7th level or lower that are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), flanking, or using surprise attack.
**Evasion** When the gang leader rolls a success on a Reflex save, they get a critical success instead.
**Surprise Attack** On the first round of combat, if the gang leader rolls [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) for initiative, creatures who haven't acted are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the gang leader.
**Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** The gang leader is targeted with an attack by an attacker they can see; **Effect** The gang leader gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** shortsword +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d6+10 piercing **Ranged** sling +18 ([Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+8 bludgeoning **Brutal Rally** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Trigger** The gang leader rolls a critical hit against a creature; **Effect** All allies that can see the gang leader gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls until the start of the gang leader's next turn. **Gang Up** Any enemy is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the gang leader's melee attacks due to flanking as long as the enemy is within melee reach of both the gang leader and one of the gang leader's allies. **Quick Draw** The gang leader Interacts to draw a weapon, then Strikes with that weapon. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The gang leader deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
Gang Structure A gang leader might run a gang, but several other NPCs in this chapter make for good gang members. A gang of significant size typically has a pyramid structure so that only a few members report directly to the boss and it's harder to link crimes directly to those in charge if someone gets arrested.
name: Gang Leader

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Charming Scoundrel](/NPCs.aspx?ID=965)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

_This handsome gnome sports an impressive coiffure and a flashy costume that catches the eye._

While most Firebrands enjoy the attention their exploits bring, the charming scoundrel takes this to extremes, often boasting about their daring exploits and attempting to parlay stories of their notorious deeds into free drinks or lodging. The scoundrel sees little harm in taking credit for any activities attributed to the organization, provided that intervention from local law enforcement seems unlikely. They are likely to take on high-profile jobs for the Firebrands just for the thrill of later recounting the narrow scrapes of their adventure.

 The charming scoundrel is as dramatic in combat as outside of it, often darting from opponent to opponent and disorienting foes with flashy swordplay. If a battle is going well, the scoundrel will often shout taunts at slow-footed opponents or encouragement to allies. While prideful and competitive, the scoundrel is a pragmatist at heart and will opt to add a tale of a cunning escape to their repertoire if the odds seem stacked against them.

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Vanton Finch</title>

Vanton is a bold and dashing rogue, but he also considers himself a romantic at heart. He can never resist a chance to show off for beautiful onlookers, even when his bravado gets him into trouble. Vanton makes sure to keep up with society, and has a cursory knowledge of all the most fashionable plays, songs, and most importantly, love sonnets. Though he may seem like nothing more than a shallow performer with an overstuffed ego, Vanton Finch can be truly brutal in a fight if his life is on the line—the same flippant tricks he uses to put on a show in front of bystanders can also be used as deadly feints and unfair combat tactics.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Privateer Captain](/NPCs.aspx?ID=966)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

_A faint crust of salt rimes the coattails of this mariner, and her steely eyes gaze from beneath the brim of her tricorne hat._

 Dismissively called a pirate or thief by some, the privateer captain commands a loyal crew that plies the waters of the Inner Sea and beyond. Privateers work for their own profit and reputation, of course, but they also commit themselves to keeping the sea-lanes free from navies that strive to enforce land law on the unbounded waves. Tyrants and slavers are their favorite targets, and many a greedy colonizer or trafficker in flesh has been sent to the bottom of the sea after the captain liberated their cargo. Generally, however, the privateer captain is willing to spare prisoners so that they might be ransomed and spread tales of Firebrands' actions across the region.

 The privateer captain is often at the vanguard of boarding parties. Many call on the power of the fickle goddess Besmara to strengthen their crew in battle and sanctify each shipboard raid. As at home in water as on deck, a privateer captain will gladly leap into the sea to pursue a foe or rescue a fallen crewmate. Though the privateer captain might run under a false flag or attempt to deceive a foe before battle, they generally honor agreements to surrender and make a point to keep their word, particularly when dealing with other Firebrands.

As is standard for increasing spell ranges, if the spell normally has a range of touch, extend its range to 30 feet.

**Sink or Swim** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The privateer captain exerts their influence on a surrounding body of water. For 1 minute, if an ally within 60 feet rolls a success on an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39), they get a critical success instead, and if any enemy within 60 feet rolls a failure on an Athletics check to Swim, it gets a critical failure instead. For the ability's duration, enemies within 60 feet of the privateer captain treat the water as turbulent (if it was not already rougher), requiring an Athletics check DC of 20 to Swim, or the water's usual DC, whichever is higher.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Stella Fane</title>

Captain Stella Fane was the victim of a greed-fueled mutiny when a Chelaxian slaving ring bought her crew and first mate out from under her. No one is certain how she survived—many whisper that she didn't, and the Stella who returned to the seas is actually a ghost. The rumors aren't true, but Stella doesn't correct them. Now armed with a new ship and crew, Stella is on a mission of vengeance against her former shipmates, the slavers of Cheliax, and anyone else who dares support either.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hellknight Armiger](/NPCs.aspx?ID=967)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

_This menacing dwarf wears a suit of armor emblazoned with the symbol of her Hellknight order, and she wields a mighty flail._

 Armigers are the foot soldiers of the Hellknights and form the largest group of members for most orders. Whatever their specific reasons for joining the organization, armigers share the Hellknights' dedication to spreading the rule of law across Golarion. This commitment sees them serving in a variety of missions, whether it be rooting out bandits and crooks, imprisoning smugglers and black marketeers, or investigating rumors of sedition. While frontier villagers sometimes welcome the assurance of safety that the Hellknights bring, free thinkers and historians chafe at the restrictions on movement and expression that armigers enforce.

 Although armigers are not yet full knights, those who underestimate them have made a grievous error. Once ordered to take a position or eliminate a foe, an armiger is unerring in fulfilling their order. During battle, they wade eagerly into the fray, targeting servants of chaotic gods and those they observe using underhanded tactics.

</column>

**Damage** 1d8+4 piercing

**Weapon Mastery**   When the Hellknight armiger scores a critical hit with their war flail, their target is knocked prone.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Myra Bloodforge</title>

Myra felt damned even before she heard of the Hellknights. Her clan was disgraced, stripped of its true name, and cast out from the Five King's Mountains before she was born, for reasons unknown. Many dwarves believe the Bloodforge clan to be cursed, and it's certainly true that many of her family have suffered terrible fates. Abandoned by her kin, Myra turned to the Order of the Gate instead, intending to call forth fiends and practice dark magic to enforce order in the world, and perhaps to break the ill fate that holds her family hostage.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hellknight Paravicar](/NPCs.aspx?ID=968)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

_Face covered by a menacing helm and body clad in ornate plate, this figure projects an aura of immutable authority_.

 Each order of Hellknights is led by a powerful paravicar, a general in charge of dictating the order's tactics and the administration of its resources. Within the order, the word of these figures is law, and members from the lowliest squire to the most senior knight follow commands from a paravicar with unswerving devotion. Paravicars see their personal dedication to the order's edicts as an example for all members and hold themselves to the same high standards as their underlings. Though they seldom need to call upon them, paravicars use their considerable infernal powers to reinforce their positions in the Hellknight's hierarchy.

 In combat, paravicars take direct command of their troops of knights and armigers. Paravicars are experienced campaigners who prefer calculated tactical gambits to hot-headed, risky maneuvers that could leave their forces exposed. If pressed into combat, paravicars use their abilities to demoralize their foes and compel enemies to become their allies or to flee the field.

[Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=55), [Spellwrack](/Spells.aspx?ID=298), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=301), [Summon Fiend](/Spells.aspx?ID=323), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) (3 slots)

**Sorcerer Bloodline Spells** DC 30, 3 Focus Points
- **6th**
[Diabolic Edict](/Spells.aspx?ID=498), [Embrace the Pit](/Spells.aspx?ID=506), [Hellfire Plume](/Spells.aspx?ID=513)

**Steady Spellcasting**   If a reaction would disrupt the Hellknight paravicar's spellcasting, they can attempt a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted.

**Universal Obedience** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) If the next action the Hellknight paravicar uses is to Cast a sorcerer Spell with the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) trait, the spell affects its target as if the target and the Hellknight paravicar shared a common language. The target must have a language.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Sergia Novellia</title>

The battle to enforce order is the driving force behind all Hellknights, but it's more personal for Sergia Novellia. In her early days as an armiger, she and her companions accidentally fell through a rift to the Plane of Shadow and fell prey to the cruel ministrations of the velstracs. Though eventually rescued by a pair of Nidalese shadowcallers, Sergia returned with scars on both mind and body. She now hides her features behind terrifying armor, though she actually delights in the gruesome appearance it gives her. Sergia serves the rigid Order of the Godclaw to enforce her iron control on both society and herself.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-200" />
name: Hellknight Paravicar

_With his well-worn plate armor and mighty shield, this tall figure appears ready to stand against any foe._ Even with the fall of the nation of Lastwall, many people still dedicate their lives to opposing the forces and schemes of the Whispering Tyrant. Styling themselves the Shining Sentinels, they see themselves as a bulwark against the further spread of the Tyrant's influence. When not rallying troops to battle against their sworn foe or protecting villages from undead incursions, these knights travel the Inner Sea in hopes of recruiting other righteous souls to fight against the undead hordes. Many Shining Sentinels are servants of good deities, such as Iomedae; in battle, they turn their god-given powers against their foes. These virtuous warriors put the safety of their companions before their own and often hold defensive positions to protect their allies. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Virtuous Defender](/NPCs.aspx?ID=969) **Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 122 **Perception** +10 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44) **Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +9, [Shining Crusade Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +9 **Str** +4 **Dex** +0 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Composite Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74) (20 arrows), [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13), [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 7, HP 30, BT 15) --- **AC** 22 (24 with shield raised) **Fort** +12 **Ref** +6 (+9 vs. damaging effects) **Will** +12 **HP** 70 **Shield Ally** The virtuous defender's shield is studier in their hands. The statistics for the shield above include this benefit; it's a normal steel shield for anyone else.
**[Retributive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=31)** The virtuous defender can Step to put the foe in reach before making a melee Retributive Strike.
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** longsword +12 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d8+6 slashing **Ranged** composite longbow +8 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)), **Damage** 1d8+4 piercing **Champion Devotion Spells** DC 19, 1 Focus Point - **2nd** [Lay on Hands](/Spells.aspx?ID=395), [Veil of Confidence](/Spells.aspx?ID=466)
Avill Ransford When Avill's sister chose the path of the crusader, Avill was supportive, eagerly reading all of his sister's letters about her training and religious studies. The final missive Avill received was bursting with pride, as his sister reported that she had been assigned to Vigil. Only months later, Tar-Baphon destroyed the city. Avill now wields a sword in his sister's honor and in his sister's place.
name: Virtuous Defender

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Veteran Reclaimer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=970)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

_This grizzled half-orc wields a longbow with the familiarity of a woman who considers it more as an extension of her body than a weapon._

 When Lastwall fell, many of the former Knights of Ozem became disheartened and scattered, wandering aimlessly through the wilds of the Inner Sea. Though the horrific events that led to the fall of Lastwall shook their faith in the traditional hierarchy of the Knights, they hardened their resolve to hunt all undead from the face of Golarion. These Crimson Reclaimers still see the Whispering Tyrant as their primary foe, but they can be found in many of the most haunted regions of the world, living off the land and hunting the restless dead.

 While heroism and fervor for their cause still burns in their heart, veteran reclaimers have lived long enough to lose their taste for tales of courtly valor and valiant last stands. They have learned the value of caution and thus engage their enemies from range and use terrain to their advantage. They are quick to strike back at enemies that harm their allies, knowing staunch teammates are essential in the face of the world's many horrors.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_bastard sword_ +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)),
**Damage** 2d8+8 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_composite longbow_ +22 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)),

**Crimson Vengeance** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The veteran reclaimer is wielding a ranged weapon with [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254). **Effect** The veteran reclaimer makes two Strikes with the required weapon against a target that has attacked the veteran reclaimer or one of their allies within the last round (the multiple attack penalty applies normally). If both hit the creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

**Nature's Edge**   Enemies are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the veteran reclaimer in areas of natural difficult terrain or in areas of difficult terrain resulting from a [snare](/Traits.aspx?ID=146).

**Vengeful Edge**   When attacking a creature that attacked the veteran reclaimer or one of their allies within the last round, the veteran reclaimer has a multiple attack penalty of –3, or –2 with their dagger.

**Weapon Mastery**   When the veteran reclaimer critically hits a creature who attacked the veteran reclaimer or one of their allies within the last round, the reclaimer applies the weapon's critical specialization effect.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Ruthal Ironroar</title>

The constant battles between Belkzen and Lastwall resulted in prejudice on both sides, and Ruthal never found her life in either land pleasant. For many years, Ruthal lived alone in the wilderness, avoiding other people. Yet when Lastwall began to crumble, the half-orc found she couldn't turn her head the other way. Though her moods are capricious and her social skills out of practice, Ruthal is a true angel to those that she rescues.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Rain-Scribe](/NPCs.aspx?ID=971)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

_This sharp-eyed half-elf wears weather-beaten gear that would easily blend in with any natural terrain, and carries an elaborately carved walking staff._

 When a group of adepts leaves the Magaambya, whether to investigate a magical phenomenon, conduct a diplomatic mission, or visit scholars in a distant land, a Rain-Scribe often serves as their guide. An expert in travel through difficult environments, the Rain-Scribe provides for their companions while helping them avoid the many hazards of travel in the Inner Sea. Rain-Scribes are some of the most well-traveled members of the academy, and most have secured alliances or promises of safe passage through the lands where they walk.

 Should the Rain-Scribe or their companions be attacked on their journeys, the scribe is a capable combatant who strives to turn their communion with the natural world into their greatest advantage in battle. Whether by using primal magic against their foes or striking unseen from the underbrush, the Rain-Scribe assures that no opponent threatens their traveling companions for long.

<column gap="tiny">

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 19

The Rain-Scribe must end their movement [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) or behind cover to remain [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).

<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Kamou Odhiambo</title>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Tempest-Sun Mage](/NPCs.aspx?ID=972)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

_A wooden mask carved with a stoic leopard's features covers the face of this scholar. Her long cloak is embroidered with the names of fellow tribe members._

 The Tempest-Sun Mages are the chosen protectors of the Magaambya academy and the lands that surround it, watching from the skies for any sign of incursion approaching from the surrounding jungle. They are known as much for the close bonds that they form with their companions as for their extensive knowledge of magical forces, and many mages inscribe the names of important allies on their clothing or gear as reminders of the reason for their vigilance and dedication. The diligence and power of the Tempest-Sun Mages has ensured that no invading force has ever come within 20 miles of the university they defend.

 When the Tempest-Sun Mages are not defending the academy itself or accompanying envoys or teachers to distant locales, they use their mastery of arcane and primal magic to keep watch in the surrounding city and beyond, keeping an eye on the balance of power throughout the whole of the Mwangi Expanse. This magical intelligence often allows them to foresee threats to the academy or any nearby towns before such threats can even organize and approach.

While Tempest-Sun Mages generally prefer to disable or incapacitate enemies, and readily call upon on their companions from the Uzunjati to reason with foes when possible, they do not hesitate to unleash lethal force against dangerous or implacable enemies, often taking to the air and raining down elemental fury. In particular, these mages have little tolerance for demons, having previously suffered attacks from the worshippers of the demon lord [Angazhan](/Deities.aspx?ID=72) who inhabit the city of Usaro.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 28

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

**Will** +21

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_staff of abjuration_ +16 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning

<actions string="Single Action" />
_crossbow_ +20 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8+2 piercing

**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 30, attack +20
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330)
- **1st**
[Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=53), [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=122), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143), [Unseen Servant](/Spells.aspx?ID=352)
- **2nd**
[Endure Elements](/Spells.aspx?ID=99), [False Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=108), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271)
- **3rd**
[Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289), [Stinking Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=309)

- **5th**
[Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=365)

- **6th**
[Elemental Tempest](/Spells.aspx?ID=527), [Force Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=529)

**Mask Bond** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The Tempest-Sun Mage has not acted yet on their turn; **Effect** The Tempest-Sun Mage expends the power stored in their bonded item, which is their mask. This gives them the ability to cast one spell they prepared today and already cast, without spending a spell slot. They must still Cast the Spell and meet the spell's other requirements.

**Reach Spell** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) If the next action the Tempest-Sun Mage uses is to Cast a Spell that has a range, increase that spell's range by 30 feet. As is standard for increasing spell ranges, if the spell normally has a range of touch, extend its range to 30 feet.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Tahenkot</title>

Strong-headed and never satisfied, Tahenkot has always approached her studies and ambitions with the force of a bull smashing through a wall. She prides herself on her skill at magical flight and teaches others to fly according to her exacting standards. Though her attitude can be off-putting to some, Tahenkot knows what she's worth and refuses to accept any less.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-202" />
name: Tempest-Sun Mage

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pathfinder Field Agent](/NPCs.aspx?ID=973)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

_This half-elf woman looks dressed to travel anywhere and prepared for any eventuality she might find along the way._

 No two field agents follow the same path to join the Pathfinder Society, nor the same path once they embark on their first expedition. The Society's treacherous missions require all types of adventurers, from seasoned combatants to ruin delvers and experts in the occult. Some field agents are highly specialized, filling a single role in a larger group of Pathfinders, while others branch out into a broad range of abilities.

 This field agent seeks the thrill of discovering lost treasures from the Inner Sea's many ancient ruins. She scouts ahead for her team, keeping an eye out for enemies, traps, and other dangers. In fights, she is cautious and practical, preferring to stay mobile and out of sight. She sneaks in for a finishing blow when victory is certain.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Pathfinder Field Agent](/NPCs.aspx?ID=973)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Elf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=58" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 126

**Perception** +11; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), trap finder

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +6, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +6, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +6, [Pathfinder Society Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +8, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Scout's Warning** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The field agent is about to roll a Perception check for initiative. **Effect** The field agent audibly or visually warns their allies of imminent danger. This ability gains the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait if the warning is audible, and the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait if the warning is visual. Any ally who is able to hear the agent (if the warning is audible) or see the agent (if the warning is visual) gains a +1 circumstance bonus to their initiative roll.

**Items**
[Comprehension Elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=88), [_minor healing potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=186), [Infiltrator's Elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=93), [Rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36), [Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=77) (20 arrows), [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5), [Thieves' Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 (22 against traps)

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +11 

+1 circumstance to all saves vs. traps
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

**Deny Advantage** The field agent isn't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to flanking, [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) creatures of 4th level or lower, or to creatures of 4th-level or lower using surprise attack.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rapier +12 ([deadly 1d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortbow +12 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 piercing

**Mobility**   When the field agent takes a Stride action to move half their Speed or less, that movement does not trigger reactions.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   2d6

**Surprise Attack**   On the first round of combat, any creatures that haven't acted yet are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the field agent.

**Trap Finder**   The field agent has an intuitive sense that alerts them to the dangers and presence of traps. They gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to find traps, to AC against attacks made by traps, and to saves against traps. Even if they aren't [Searching](/Activities.aspx?ID=9), they still get a check to find traps. They can disable traps that require a proficiency rank of master in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Siora Qinndove</title>

An heir to two wealthy families, Siora grew up used to getting her own way. But Siora wound up becoming interested in archeology and the act of investigating every nook and cranny of an unknown artifact. Seeing that lost cities were hard to find and often contained things money couldn't buy, Siora joined the Pathfinder Society, loaded down with every piece of expensive gear that could prove useful.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-203" />
name: Pathfinder Field Agent

_The barnacle-crusted armor of this dwarf hints at a thousand exciting tales._ The venture-captains of the Pathfinder Society rose through the ranks of the organization by following a unique path. An individual venture-captain has overcome numerous challenges, and each has countless stories to tell—and the scars to go with them. Each venture-captain maintains a lodge somewhere in the Inner Sea, where Pathfinder agents can rest, share stories, and set out on new missions. The venture-captain assigns these missions themselves, and they can draw on the significant resources of the Pathfinder Society—as well as any local allies they have—to ensure the missions' success. This venture-captain operates a lodge that also serves as a tavern. His interest in epic tales led him to join the Society, and he gladly shares these stories with interested parties. Opportunistic field agents can gain the venture-captain's favor by recovering unique and valuable liquors. The venture-captain hopes to one day publish a Pathfinder Chronicle of his own. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Pathfinder Venture-Captain](/NPCs.aspx?ID=974) **Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 127 **Perception** +20 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Alcohol Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +20, [Bardic Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +16, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +19 **Str** +5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74)_, [_+1 half plate_](/Armor.aspx?ID=12), [_minor sturdy shield_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=327) (Hardness 8, HP 64, BT 32), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=47)_, [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) --- **AC** 32 (34 with shield raised) **Fort** +22 (successes are instead critical successes) **Ref** +18 **Will** +19 **HP** 195 **Unshakable** Anytime the venture-captain gains the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition, reduce its value by 1.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _warhammer_ +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 2d8+11 bludgeoning **Ranged** _composite longbow_ +20 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)), **Damage** 2d8+8 piercing **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 27 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Inspire Competence](/Spells.aspx?ID=385), [Inspire Courage](/Spells.aspx?ID=386), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) **Bardic Lore** The venture-captain can [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) on any topic using their [Bardic Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=179) skill. **Paragon's Guard** ([Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=152)) While the venture-captain is in this stance and wielding a shield, their shield is always raised. **Weapon Mastery** The venture-captain has spent hours training with the hammer. They gain access to the critical specialization effect of all simple and martial hammers.
Khoumrock Blackthane The epitome of a salty sea dog, Khoumrock has experienced a lot in his tenure as a Pathfinder. Sunk and trapped beneath the waves, kidnapped by [alghollthus](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=1), rescued from alghollthus—if there's an adventure to be found underwater, Khoumrock has experienced it. These days, Khoumrock prefers to keep his feet on solid ground, though he's always eager to share his stories to wide-eyed new recruits. For all his hardships, nothing has ever matched the wonders and thrills of the mysterious world he traversed below the ocean, and Khoumrock is happy to help others explore that incredible place—and live to tell tales of their adventures.
name: Pathfinder Venture-Captain

<title level="1" pfs="">[Deghuun (Children Of Mhar)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=975)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Mhar, an ancient evil deity of caverns, mountains, and volcanoes, belongs to the pantheon of the Great Old Ones—monstrous beings of alien intellect and unfathomable power. The so-called children of Mhar (known as deghuuns in the chaotic tongues of Mhar's enraptured cultists) physically resemble blue-gray octopods made of stone, mud, and molten iron. Venthole-like craters dot the surface of a deghuun's body, through which the creature can gaze out with a single golden eye that it can move from crater to crater.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 40

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Deghuun.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 18">[Children Of Mhar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=975)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 78

**Perception** +30; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), smoke vision

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +9

**Dex** +6

**Con** +8

**Int** -1

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Smoke Vision** The deghuun ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke or smog, including from Sulfurous Plume.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 42 

**Fort** +34 

**Ref** +30 

**Will** +26 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 420

</row>

**Immunities**
fire, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)

**Resistances**
physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271))

**Weaknesses**
cold 10, good 10

**Unfathomable Aspect** Anytime a deghuun attempts a Will save against an [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) spell, the spell's caster must attempt a DC 38 Will save or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for up to the spell's duration.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d10+17 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
lava ball +34 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 4d6+17 bludgeoning plus 3d6 fire

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 38
- **6th**
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94) (×3), [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144)
- **8th**
[Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=95)

**Beak Crunch** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The deghuun has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) with its tentacles; **Effect** The creature takes 4d8+17 piercing damage.

**Sulfurous Plume** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) The deghuun vents caustic smog for 1d4 rounds. Any creature that ends its turn within 30 feet of the deghuun during this time takes 6d6 fire damage and is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (DC 38 Fortitude save; the creature avoids the sickened condition on a critical success, or is sickened 2 on a critical failure). While sickened in this way, a creature takes a –2 status penalty to its saving throw against the deghuun's [_hallucination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=144). Any creature that's in the smog is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) and treats other creatures as concealed. Strong winds dissipate the smog.

**Tentacle Assault** <actions string="Single Action" />  The deghuun makes three tentacle Strikes, each against a different creature within reach. These attacks count toward its multiple attack penalty as normal.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Scions of the Elder Mythos</title>

Children of Mhar are not the only agents of the Great Old Ones and Outer Gods to emerge on Golarion. [Gibbering mouthers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=227) supposedly have some connection to unholy Abhoth, and the ancestors of the Darklands-dwelling chardas were once potent clerics of Orgesh, the Faceless God.
</aside>
name: Children Of Mhar

Juggernauts of destruction, vavakias are formed from the souls of mortals who extract, enslave, or consume the souls of others. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Vavakia](/Monsters.aspx?ID=976) **Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 79 **Perception** +32; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +34, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +32 **Str** +9 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 **Items** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [ranseur](/Weapons.aspx?ID=35)_ --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +28 **Will** +30 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 350 **Weaknesses** cold iron 15, good 15 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 60 feet, DC 37
**Restoration Vulnerability** A vavakia is repulsed by the restoration of souls. The demon takes 3d10+10 mental damage the first time each round that a creature within 100 feet of it is restored to life or loses the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) condition. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** _ranseur_ +36 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 piercing plus 1d10 acid and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** jaws +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+17 piercing plus 2d8 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and smoking wound **Melee** claw +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 slashing **Melee** tail +35 ([Backswing](/Traits.aspx?ID=173), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+17 bludgeoning plus stunning tail **Divine Innate Spells** DC 40 - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) - **8th** [Divine Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=81), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) (at will), [Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=95) - **9th** [Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=186), [Power Word Stun](/Spells.aspx?ID=228) - **Constant (9th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Rituals** DC 40 - **1st** [Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21) **Smoking Wound** A creature damaged by the vavakia's jaws is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) from the rank smell of vapors from the wound. **Soulfire Breath** ([Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) The vavakia exhales ghostly green fire in a 60-foot cone. Living creatures in the area take 20d6 negative damage (DC 42 basic Reflex save). A good creature that fails this save is becomes [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute (stupefied 4 on a critical failure). A creature slain by Soulfire Breath can't be resurrected except by powerful magic such as a [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) spell. The vavakia can't use Soulfire Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Soulfire Inhalation** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirements** The vavakia's last action was Soulfire Breath; **Effect** The vavakia inhales and regains 10 Hit Points for each creature damaged by its Soulfire Breath. Any excess healing is gained as temporary Hit Points, which remain for 1 minute. **Stunning Tail** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) If the vavakia critically hits with its tail Strike, the target must succeed at a DC 40 Fortitude save or be [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) for 1 round (1d4 rounds on a critical failure). **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Large or smaller, claw, DC 40
Saurian Tyrants Vavakias carve out tyrannical empires throughout the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13), particularly in steaming jungles and fetid swamps. Their minions are usually lesser demons, [xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102), or [saurians](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=204). They enjoy coming to the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) to feed on souls and spread devastation.
name: Vavakia

<title level="1" pfs="">[Saurian Warmonger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=977)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These saurians adopt warfare as their primary role and greatest calling. They particularly excel at taking slaves.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 37

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/Saurian_SaurianWarmonger.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 16">[Saurian Warmonger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=977)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Dinosaur" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=45" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 80

**Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +32, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +30, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +30

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +5

**Con** +6

**Int** +4

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Items**
[Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) mancatcher_, _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 39 

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +28 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 340

</row>

**Resistances**
fire 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_spear_ +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d6+16 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_mancatcher_ +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d6+16 slashing plus restrain

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +30 ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d10+16 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 3d6+16 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_spear_ +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 3d6+16 piercing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 37
- **3rd**
[Animal Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=12) (at will; dinosaurs only)
- **4th**
[Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6)
- **Constant (2nd)**
[Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293)

**Boneshaking Roar** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The saurian warmonger bellows, evoking the power of the primordial world. Non-[dinosaur](/Traits.aspx?ID=45) creatures within 30 feet take 10d6 sonic damage (DC 34 basic Fortitude save). On a failed save, a creature is also [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round (stunned 1d4 on a critical failure). The saurian can't use Boneshaking Roar again for 1d4 rounds.

**Primordial Balance**   A warmonger wields their mancatcher in one hand.

**Restrain**   When the warmonger makes a successful mancatcher Strike, their target must succeed at a DC 34 Reflex save or become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) until the end of the warmonger's next turn. Each time the creature fails an attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) the warmonger, it takes 2d6 piercing damage.

**Three-Limbed Lunge** <actions string="Single Action" />  The saurian warmonger makes three Strikes: one Strike each with the weapons in their hands and one Strike with their tail. They can perform these attacks in any order, though each counts toward their multiple attack penalty as normal. After making a Three-Limbed Lunge, the warmonger is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the beginning of their next turn.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Saurian Worldwatcher](/Monsters.aspx?ID=978)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The longest-lived saurians favor introspection and take the long view of things—though this thoughtfulness should not be mistaken for weakness. Other saurians defer to worldwatchers' experience.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 40

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35
</column>

</column>

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +35, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +32, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +34, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +34

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +9

**Con** +6

**Int** +5

**Wis** +8

**Cha** +6
</row>

[javelins](/Weapons.aspx?ID=71) (2), _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [naginata](/Weapons.aspx?ID=98)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Ref** +33 

**Will** +32

<row gap="medium">

**Raptor Jaw Disarm** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** A creature makes a melee Strike against the worldwatcher but misses; **Effect** The worldwatcher breaks the triggering creature's limb or weapon. If the triggering Strike was an unarmed attack, the triggering creature takes 6d6 bludgeoning damage and becomes [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) until the limb is reset (an Interact action with the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait). If the triggering Strike was a weapon Strike, the weapon takes 6d6 bludgeoning damage.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_naginata_ +35 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 3d8+19 slashing plus stunning blow

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +33 ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d10+17 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d10+19 bludgeoning plus stunning blow

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 3d6+19 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
javelin +35 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 1d6+19 piercing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 40
- **2nd**
[Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=11)
- **3rd**
[Animal Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=12) (at will; dinosaurs only)
- **4th**
[Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6)
- **9th**
[Fiery Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=115), [Summon Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) (dinosaur only), [Volcanic Eruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=360)
- **Constant (2nd)**
[Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293)

**Boneshaking Roar** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The saurian worldwatcher bellows, evoking the power of the primordial world. Non-[dinosaur](/Traits.aspx?ID=45) creatures within 30 feet take 10d6 sonic damage (DC 38 basic Fortitude save). On a failed save, a creature is also [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round (stunned 1d4 on a critical failure). The saurian can't use Boneshaking Roar again for 1d4 rounds.

**Stunning Blow**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) Whenever the worldwatcher hits a creature with their fist or naginata Strike, that creature must succeed at a DC 39 Fortitude save or be [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (stunned 3 on a critical hit).

</column>
