Exceptionally efficient and utterly merciless, clockwork assassins carry out their murderous directives with mechanical precision. Though they can disguise themselves, upon close inspection, they're plainly artificial, with just a single eye and weapons integrated into their arms. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Clockwork Assassin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1004) **Source** [Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven](/Sources.aspx?ID=49) pg. 82 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), smoke vision **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27 **Str** +6 **Dex** +8 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 **Integrated Launcher** A projectile launcher is integrated into a clockwork assassin's systems, containing 10 spinning blades and five smoke bombs. When the assassin is destroyed, the launcher and its ammunition are also destroyed.
**Smoke Vision** The clockwork assassin ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke.
**Wind-Up** For the clockwork assassin to act, it must be wound with a unique key by another creature. This takes 1 minute. Once wound, it remains operational for 24 hours, after which time it becomes unaware of its surroundings and can't act until it's wound again.
The assassin can enter standby mode as a 3-action activity. Its operational time doesn't decrease in standby, but it can sense its surroundings (with a –2 penalty to Perception). It can't act, with one exception: when it perceives a creature, it can exit standby as a reaction (rolling initiative if appropriate).
A creature can attempt a DC 31 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) to wind the assassin down. For each success, the assassin loses 1 hour of operational time. This can be done even if the assassin is in standby mode. --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +29 **Will** +19 **HP** 230 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poisoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or orichalcum) **Weaknesses** electricity 15, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378) 15 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** rapier hand +27 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d6+12 piercing plus unbalancing blow **Ranged** spinning blade +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10+9 slashing **Rapid Repair** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The clockwork assassin spends 1 hour of its operational time to repair itself, regaining 25 Hit Points and refilling its integrated projectile launcher with 4 spinning blades (to a maximum of 10) and 2 smoke bombs (to a maximum of 5). **Smoke Bomb Launcher** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The clockwork assassin launches a smoke bomb. A screen of thick, opaque smoke fills a 10-foot burst within 60 feet of the clockwork assassin. Creatures within the area are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all other creatures are concealed to them. The smoke lasts for 1 minute or until dispersed by a strong wind. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The clockwork assassin's Strikes deal an additional 3d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Unbalancing Blow** Creatures hit by the clockwork assassin's rapier hand Strike are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of the clockwork assassin's next turn.
Assassin Augmentations Clockwork assassins can feature a variety of abilities and mechanisms to better complete their assigned tasks. Assassins expecting to face large groups of enemies have integrated weapons that target multiple foes at once. Other assassins have magical integration, allowing them to cast useful spells.
name: Clockwork Assassin

<title level="1" pfs="">[Jorogumo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1005)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Appearing as beautiful, well-dressed humans, jorogumos lurk in high mountains and prey on travelers. These evil creatures often keep giant spiders as pets and can fully change into a giant spider or sprout spider legs from their backs. Jorogumos usually eat their prey, but some humanoids meet an even more gruesome fate as living incubators for jorogumo eggs.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Con** +5

**Cha** +7
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Darting Legs** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Requirement** The jorogumo has their spider legs extended or has Changed Shape; **Trigger** The jorogumo is targeted with an attack; **Effect** The jorogumo raises a leg, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, swim 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +25,
**Damage** 2d8+14 piercing plus jorogumo venom

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 2d6+14 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
web +23 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** Web Trap

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 34
- **1st**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (at will)
- **3rd**
[Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) (at will)

- **7th**
[Summon Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) (spiders only)
- **Constant (2nd)**
[Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293) (spiders only)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The jorogumo takes on the appearance of any Small or Medium spider. This doesn't change their Speed or Strikes.

**Jorogumo Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 32; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 poison damage and stupefied 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 2d4 hours

**Spider Legs** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Requirement** The jorogumo is in humanoid form; **Effect** Eight large spider legs sprout from the jorogumo's back, granting them a 40-foot climb Speed and allowing them to use the Darting Legs reaction.

**Web Trap**   A creature hit by the jorogumo's web attack is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and stuck to the nearest surface, preventing the creature from moving. The DC to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) or [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) the web trap is 32.

</column>
name: Jorogumo

markdown:
Shatterlings have a fixation on mirrors that gives them a strange affinity for reflective surfaces. These creeping, wicked fey stand barely 3 feet high and look like hunched humanoids made of shifting panes of jagged mirror glass. Shatterlings usually inhabit areas with plenty of mirrors, like carnival fun houses, opulent manors, or playhouses. Capricious and malevolent, shatterlings quickly kill or scare away anyone else in such areas, as they desire to be the only ones to gaze into the mirrors they claim as their own. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Shatterling](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1006) **Source** [Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven](/Sources.aspx?ID=49) pg. 84 **Perception** +26; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), mirror senses **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +32, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +30 **Str** +5 **Dex** +8 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Mirror Senses** A shatterling can see and hear through any unbroken mirror within 100 feet. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +30 **Will** +26 **HP** 305 **Immunities** bleed **Weaknesses** bludgeoning 15 **Attack of Opportunity**
**Extra Reaction** A shatterling gains an additional reaction each turn, but can use this extra reaction only for Divert Strike.
**Divert Strike** **Trigger** A shatterling takes non-bludgeoning damage and there's an unbroken mirror at least 1 foot wide within 100 feet of the shatterling; **Effect** The nearest mirror to the shatterling at least 1 foot wide breaks, and the damage to the shatterling is reduced to 0. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** mirror hand +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d6+11 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** mirror dart +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d6+11 piercing **Blood-Fueled Titter** If the shatterling attempts an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) a target within 30 feet of a creature with [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), the shatterling has +4 circumstance bonus to the Intimidation check. In addition, the shatterling takes no penalty to the check for not speaking a language the target can hear, and the target isn't temporarily immune to the shatterling's attempts to Demoralize it unless the shatterling critically fails its Intimidation check. **Mirror Jump** ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) During this Stride, the shatterling can move into any unbroken mirror at least 1 foot wide and out of another unbroken mirror at least 1 foot wide within 100 feet of the first mirror, treating the mirrors as connected. The shatterling can move through any number of pairs of foot-wide mirrors during its mirror jump, requiring each pair to be within 100 feet of each other while doing so. **Vanish in Reflections** The shatterling doesn't appear in reflective surfaces if it doesn't want to. When it's within 100 feet of an unbroken mirror at least 1 foot wide, a shatterling can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) even without cover or without being concealed. **Wipe Away Cracks** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The shatterling touches a broken mirror, instantly repairing it.
Drawn to Mirrors A half-dimension exists just behind mirrors in the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), although scholars disagree whether it's a plane of its own or simply a strange reflection of the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) or [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9). In any event, shatterlings sometimes see this realm when they leap through mirrors but can never reach it, an inaccessibility that fuels their hatred. Many shatterlings spend their days leaping between mirrors, hoping to catch elusive glimpses of the mirrorworld they will never touch.
name: Shatterling

The Stabbing Beast, the divine representative of [Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=13), resembles an immense scorpion with a soft and strangely captivating voice. They appear on Golarion to bury secrets, support the faithful, or aid audacious thefts.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/StabbingBeast.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 15">[The Stabbing Beast](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1007)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
<trait label="Herald" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=330" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven](/Sources.aspx?ID=49) pg. 85

**Perception** +27; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Languages**
[Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Norgorber Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +28, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +8

**Con** +4

**Int** +3

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +3
</row>

**True Appearance** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The Stabbing Beast is transformed with Change Shape and rolls for initiative; **Effect** The Stabbing Beast ends Change Shape.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 38 

**Fort** +27 

**Ref** +29 

**Will** +27 

+2 status to all saves vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 275

</row>

**Immunities**
[poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)

**Resistances**
acid 15

**Weaknesses**
good 15

**Attack of Opportunity** <actions string="Reaction" /> Stinger only.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pincer +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d8+10 slashing plus 2d8 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
stinger +29 ([fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d8+14 piercing plus Stabbing Beast venom

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 3d6+10 piercing plus 2d8 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
venom stream +29 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 5d10 poison plus blinding stream

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 36
- **4th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (×3), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (at will)
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (×3), [Summon Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) (×3; scorpions only)
- **6th**
[Modify Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=200) (×3)
- **Constant (7th)**
[True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Blinding Stream**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) A creature hit by the Stabbing Beast's venom stream must succeed at a DC 33 Fortitude save or become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1d4 rounds.

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The Stabbing Beast takes on the appearance of a Medium humanoid with a scorpion's tail and chitinous, full-body plate armor. This doesn't change their Speed or Strikes, except that their reach becomes 5 feet and they can't make pincer Strikes.

**Conjure Swords** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) **Requirements** The Stabbing Beast is using Change Shape; **Effect** The Stabbing Beast summons one or two _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=300) [shortswords](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)_ into their hands. These weapons disappear if they leave the Stabbing Beast's grasp or if the Stabbing Beast ends Change Shape.

**Stabbing Beast Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) While a creature is enfeebled from this poison, it is [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9); **Saving Throw** DC 36; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage and [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 poison damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 6d6 poison damage and enfeebled 3 (1 round)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Heralds</title>

Nearly all deities have a specific representative known as a herald; heralds are as diverse in their appearances and abilities as the gods themselves. A creature with the herald type is always unique. The Stabbing Beast has been slain before—[Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=13) is always swift to replace it with a new Stabbing Beast, allowing the new version to retain memories of the prior version's defeats to spur the herald on to do better in its new incarnation.
</aside>
name: The Stabbing Beast

markdown:
A clockwork amalgam is a powerful construct created by applying magic to a collection of interchangeable internal mechanisms. Each clockwork amalgam has unique properties, although most stand roughly 12 feet tall and have five clockwork appendages tipped with integrated weapons. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Clockwork Amalgam](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1008) **Source** [Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale](/Sources.aspx?ID=51) pg. 81 **Perception** +36 **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38 **Str** +10 **Dex** +7 **Con** +6 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +35 **Will** +28 **HP** 455 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poisoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or orichalcum) **Weaknesses** electricity 20, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378) 20 **Efficient Winding** For the clockwork amalgam to act, it must be wound by another creature using a unique key. This takes 1 minute. Once wound, it remains operational for 72 days, after which time it becomes unaware of its surroundings and can't act until it's wound again.
The amalgam can enter standby mode as a 3-action activity. Its operational time doesn't decrease in standby, but it can sense its surroundings (with a –2 penalty to Perception). It can't act, with one exception: When it perceives a creature, it can exit standby as a reaction (rolling initiative if appropriate).
A creature can attempt a DC 31 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) to wind the clockwork down. For each success, the clockwork loses 1 hour of operational time. This can be done even if the clockwork is in standby mode.
**Recalibrate** The clockwork amalgam can recalibrate its internal structure to shed damaged components and replace them with new ones, restoring 125 HP to the construct. Each time it uses this ability, the clockwork amalgam permanently deactivates one of its five melee attacks as the components from that arm reassemble within the core of the machine. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** blade +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d12+18 slashing **Melee** blowtorch +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 10d6 fire **Melee** claw +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=25) **Melee** hose +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** stamper +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20) **Ranged** spike +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d12+8 piercing **Whirring Doom** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The clockwork amalgam becomes a blur of spinning weaponry. It makes up to five melee Strikes (one for each active melee attack it has available), taking a –4 penalty to the attack roll for each attack, but ignoring the multiple attack penalty until it has made all of its attacks. It can use each melee attack only once and it can target each creature in reach only once. If the clockwork amalgam used Whirring Doom in the previous round, it can use it again this round using only 1 action instead of 3. The amalgam's movement Speed is halved until the end of its next turn.
Fell Amalgams Some clockwork amalgams become possessed by fell spirits or fiendish influences. Gifted with a spark of sentience, these “fell amalgams” purposely seek out other clockwork creations to destroy, collecting their parts and incorporating them into the fell amalgams' own ever-growing clockwork bodies. Unlike with typical clockworks, a fell amalgam's intricate clockwork brain can hatch cunning schemes and far-reaching plans.
name: Clockwork Amalgam

markdown:
Spellcasters create elemental vessels by harvesting raw elemental energy and then binding it within a specially enchanted magic circle to grant it life. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Elemental Vessel, Water](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1009) **Source** [Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale](/Sources.aspx?ID=51) pg. 82 **Perception** +28 **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +32 **Str** +8 **Dex** +8 **Con** +8 **Int** +0 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +0 **Spell Circle** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) By performing an hour-long ritual, an elemental vessel can form a spell circle with an arcane or primal spellcaster. After the spell circle is formed, other spellcasters can join the spell circle by performing the same hour-long ritual while assisted by at least one spellcaster already in the spell circle. Each member of the spell circle must contribute at least one spell to the elemental vessel. The spellcaster permanently loses access to a spell slot equal to the spell's level and the elemental vessel adds the contributed spell to its list of arcane innate spells (regardless of the spell's original type). An elemental vessel can be imbued with no more than three spells per spell level. If the elemental vessel is destroyed, all members of the spell circle regain the spell slots they contributed to the creature. The arcane innate spells shown below represent a typical spell list for an elemental vessel, but each elemental vessel is likely to have different spells depending on what its spell circle has contributed. --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +28 **Will** +22 **HP** 225 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** fire 15 **Dispel Vulnerability** Whenever a creature successfully counteracts one of its spells, the vessel permanently loses access to that spell and takes damage equal to 4 × the spell's level. The spell circle member who contributed that spell regains access to their contributed spell slot.
**Inhabit Vessel** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) Any member of the elemental vessel's spell circle can spend 3 actions to mentally inhabit the vessel for 1 round. While they inhabit the vessel, the inhabiter controls the vessel's actions, and the vessel uses the inhabiter's Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma modifiers for all checks. Only one creature can inhabit an elemental vessel at a time. --- **Speed** 35 feet, swim 100 feet **Melee** wave +30 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+12 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=25) 10 feet **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +30 - **1st** [Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=154), [Shocking Grasp](/Spells.aspx?ID=283), [Spider Sting](/Spells.aspx?ID=300) - **2nd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210), [Touch of Idiocy](/Spells.aspx?ID=341) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=362) - **5th** [Mariner's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=184), [Wall of Ice](/Spells.aspx?ID=364) - **6th** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), [Purple Worm Sting](/Spells.aspx?ID=242) - **7th** [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), [Volcanic Eruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=360) - **8th** [Polar Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=224) **[Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14)** DC 35, no damage, [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 35, Rupture 28 **Internal Spell Strike** The elemental vessel targets an engulfed creature with one of its spells. The spell must have a range of touch.
Versatile Creations Some spellcasters favor elemental vessels because of their versatility. Their power and intelligence make them excellent guardians when not inhabited. But they can also be imbued with useful spells and inhabited from any distance, allowing spellcasters to influence events far from their location.
name: Elemental Vessel, Water

markdown:
A lusca is a predatory sea monster that resembles a three-headed shark with the body of an octopus. Ferocious electrical storms usually herald a lusca's arrival; such violent bursts of electricity result from the creature's superconductive flesh, which allows the lusca to store energy from lightning strikes that it can then discharge at foes. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Lusca](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1010) **Source** [Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale](/Sources.aspx?ID=51) pg. 83 **Perception** +31; blood scent, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 100 feet **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +31 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +9 **Int** +0 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 **Blood Scent** The lusca can smell blood in the water up to 1 mile away. --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +26 **Will** +29 **HP** 320 **Immunities** electricity **[All-Around Vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1)**
**Storm Bringer** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) When the lusca is near the surface for more than 1 hour, clouds appear and deliver a thunderstorm with a 2-mile radius centered on its location. The weather returns to normal 1 hour after the lusca departs.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** The lusca gains 2 extra reactions at the start of each of its turns that it can use only to make an Attack of Opportunity with its jaws. It can't use more than one Attack of Opportunity triggered by the same action. --- **Speed** 10 feet, swim 50 feet **Melee** jaws +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+17 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tentacle +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 bludgeoning plus lusca venom **Primal Innate Spells** DC 38 - **8th** [Punishing Winds](/Spells.aspx?ID=240) (×3) - **9th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33) (×3) **Capsize** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) The lusca tries to capsize an adjacent aquatic vessel of its size or smaller. It must succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check with a DC of 35 (reduced by 5 for each size smaller the vessel is than the lusca) or the pilot's [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) DC, whichever is higher. **Lusca Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 38; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 poison damage and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 round); **Stage 2** 6d6 poison damage and clumsy 2 (1 round) **Ravenous Jaws** The lusca makes three jaws Strikes, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the lusca's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after the lusca makes all of these attacks. If the lusca subsequently uses the [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) action, it Grabs any number of creatures it hit with Ravenous Jaws. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** jaws **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Huge, 4d12+9 bludgeoning, Rupture 38
Blue Holes Luscas frequently lurk in blue holes— marine sinkholes that descend far below the surrounding sea bottom and connect to flooded subterranean passages or deeper into the Darklands. For all their might, however, luscas don't dominate these lightless realms. Rather, they are but one of many types of crafty and titanic sea monsters, from the more-familiar [aboleths](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2) and [krakens](/Monsters.aspx?ID=275) to bizarre hybrids of whales, eels, and cuttlefish.
name: Lusca

<title level="1" pfs="">[Minchgorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1011)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An oddity even among the strange creatures of the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) of the fey, the aquatic minchgorm is a monstrous alien maw ringed with writhing tendrils. It interacts with the world through grasping, speaking simulacra that it magically shapes from water.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 43

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Minchgorm.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 18">[Minchgorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1011)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=168" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +34, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +36

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +0

**Con** +6

**Int** +5

**Cha** +8

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 42 

**Fort** +30 

**Ref** +27 

**Will** +33 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 440

</row>

**Resistances**
fire 15

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 5 feet, swim 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d10+16 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 40
- **5th**
[Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=51) (×3)
- **9th**
[Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=155)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Fast Swallow** <actions string="Reaction" />  Trigger The minchgorm [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) a creature in its jaws; Effect The minchgorm uses [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35).

**Riptide** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) Tentacles of water rise up and attempt to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) all enemies within 15 feet of the minchgorm. The minchgorm attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the Fortitude DC of each creature in the area. On a success, the creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) (Escape DC 40). On a critical success, or a success against a creature that is already grabbed, the target begins to [drown](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).

**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** <actions string="Single Action" />  Large, 3d10+16 bludgeoning, Rupture 36

**Watery Simulacra** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) The minchgorm raises up to eight figures from nearby water, each appearing in an empty space within 60 feet of the minchgorm. A simulacrum can take any shape of size Medium or smaller that the minchgorm desires, including that of a specific individual or object, but is always plainly made of water. The minchgorm can speak, cast spells, and use its senses through its simulacra, spending actions for a simulacrum on a one-for-one basis. By using a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, the minchgorm can cause any number of simulacra to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), Step, Stride, or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) up to 60 feet. A simulacrum uses the minchgorm's AC and saving throws and has 30 Hit Points. A minchgorm can have up to eight simulacra in existence at once. Simulacra dissolve immediately when more than 60 feet from the minchgorm, or if the minchgorm dies or falls unconscious. The minchgorm can dismiss any number of its simulacra as a free action.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Monstrous Esthetes</title>

Despite its aberrant appearance, a minchgorm considers itself a creature of refined tastes, exhibiting a love of poetry and other vocal performances. Solitary minchgorms often entertain themselves by staging elaborate recitals and productions cast entirely with their own simulacra.
</aside>
name: Minchgorm

The slithering, shapeless beings known as myrucarxes occasionally slip into the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) from their domains deep in the [Shadow Plane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=288), seeking to torment those who love light. Myrucarxes weave powerful illusions to draw unsuspecting victims into their squirming embrace, and they favor large cities as hunting grounds. Rather than devastate a community, a myrucarx instead prefers to snatch only a few victims to engender fear and paranoia, then retreat somewhere dark to hibernate for months or years before renewing its terrors. A myrucarx resembles a monstrous, oily slug with tentacles ending in gnashing jaws, but most victims only ever see the illusory personas it projects to lure and torment its prey. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Myrucarx](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1012) **Source** [Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale](/Sources.aspx?ID=51) pg. 85 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +34, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +31, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +34 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 **Odorless** A myrucarx emits no smell and can't be detected by [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33). --- **AC** 41 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +28 **Will** +30 **HP** 340 **Resistances** cold 20 **[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)**
**Painful Light** When attempting a save against a [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) effect, a myrucarx gets a result one degree of success lower than it rolled. A myrucarx caught in sunlight is [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3). --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +34 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+15 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 40, attack +32 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only) - **5th** [Hallucinatory Terrain](/Spells.aspx?ID=145) - **7th** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36) (at will), [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will), [Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=185) (self only), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (to [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) or [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) only) - **9th** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36), [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=96), [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237) (at will; see illusory persona) - **Constant (8th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Illusory Persona** The illusion created by the myrucarx's [_project image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=237) can resemble any Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91), including any specific individual it has seen, and has appropriate voice, scent, and mannerisms, allowing the myrucarx to use its [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) that humanoid if it chooses. **Twisting Reach** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The fanged mouths at the ends of a myrucarx's tendrils twist around obstacles. The myrucarx makes two jaws Strikes against the same target, ignoring the target's cover. Both attacks count toward the myrucarx's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks are made. The myrucarx can't [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) as its next action after Twisting Reach.
Myrucarx Cabals Myrucarxes rarely defer to any other creature, but they grudgingly acknowledge the authority of cabals of powerful myrucarxes dwelling in deep caverns on the [Shadow Plane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=288). These cabals don't bother with the mundane work of terrorizing [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) cities; instead, they engage in worlds-spanning schemes to extinguish suns and create entire planets of cold, benighted horror.
name: Myrucarx

<title level="1" pfs="">[Obrousian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1013)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Spawned from sorrow and hatred, obrousians are [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) [merfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=73) who lurk in shallow places to prey upon hated land dwellers. The genesis of an obrousian is a tragic one: some merfolk forsake their aquatic homes when they fall in love with a land dweller, only to be spurned or rejected. Having given up their world for another and then feeling that new world closed off to them as well, merfolk can become emotionally overwhelmed. When such merfolk die, often overcome with sorrow, they rise as obrousians.

 Obrousians think of little other than revenge upon the land dwellers who wronged them, and upon anyone else who crosses their path. They can contort their bodies to take the guise of other humanoids, but doing so is painful, so obrousians use this ability only when necessary to enact their schemes. Their underlying mental turmoil simmers beneath any careful preparation, and many obrousians foil their own plans with unexpected outbursts of emotion.

 Obrousians are recognizable as the merfolk they were in life, though their bodies show obvious decay and their hands have elongated into sharp claws. Their voices are melodic and entrancing, belying their rotting bodies and turbulent minds, so some obrousians hide in tall reeds or under docks before speaking to their victims, lulling them into false comfort with a pleasant voice.

<column gap="tiny">

This increases their Speed to 25 feet. It doesn't change their attack and damage modifiers with their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

**Drowning Grasp**   When a creature is grabbed by the obrousian, its lungs magically fill with water. A creature that can't breathe water is [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) while it is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20).

markdown:
Zealborn arise from mortals who become consumed by a noble or idealistic vision and choose undeath as a means of pursuing that vision. Unlike [liches](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=68), who use undeath as a path toward immortality and individual power, zealborn undergo their transformation with the total conviction that their cause is so just it should never die. They manipulate and control dozens of fanatical followers who sacrifice themselves—and rise again as powerful zeal-damned ghouls—for the zealborn's cause. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Zealborn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1014) **Source** [Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale](/Sources.aspx?ID=51) pg. 87 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +27, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +7 **Little Favors** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Each time a creature willingly performs a service, quest, or task for the zealborn, the creature takes a cumulative –1 status penalty to saving throws against the zealborn's [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) spells and abilities (maximum –4). **Items** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), [_+1 striking morningstar_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280), [sling with 20 bullets](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72) --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +22 **Will** +24 +1 status bonus to Will saves vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects **HP** 210 (rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** chaotic 10 **Rejuvenation** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When a zealborn is destroyed, it re-forms, fully healed, near a follower of its choice in 1d10 days. For the purposes of rejuvenation, a follower is defined as an intelligent creature who's well acquainted with the zealborn and is a true believer in the zealborn's mission, not merely a lukewarm supporter. A zealborn can be permanently destroyed only if they have no current followers or can be convinced to end their own life.
**Zealous Restoration** A zealborn can't regain Hit Points through resting or negative healing; they feed only on the idealism of their supporters, regaining 10 Hit Points per day for each creature under the effects of a [_geas_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) cast by the zealborn. Creatures under a zealborn's _geas_ have their maximum HP reduced by 10 each day until they are burned away to a husk and perish. After 24 hours, such creatures rise as zeal-damned [ghouls](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52) under the zealborn's control. Zeal-damned ghouls eat only the flesh of evil or chaotic creatures and deal an additional 1d6 chaotic and 1d6 negative damage with their attacks. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _morningstar_ +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d6+9 bludgeoning plus 2d6 chaotic and 2d6 negative **Ranged** sling +24 ([Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+6 bludgeoning plus 2d6 chaotic and 2d6 negative **Occult Innate Spells** DC 33 - **2nd** [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=158) (at will) - **3rd** [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104) - **4th** [Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=355) - **5th** [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=314) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Zealous Conviction](/Spells.aspx?ID=378) **Occult Rituals** DC 38 - **3rd** [Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12)
Redemption Zealborns begin with a great desire to do good but can become twisted mockeries of their ideals. When confronted with the horrors wrought by their crusades, some can be shocked out of their obsession; they can release themselves from undeath if they truly realize how far they've strayed from their original cause.
name: Zealborn

markdown:
Baatamidars are drawn to destroy planar communities on the fringes where the Outer Planes touch the chaos of the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19). They resemble floating blobs of undulating, prismatic ooze with four quivering tentacles and dribbles of oily slime that always drip upwards. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 47 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 [Baatamidar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1015) **Source** [Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege](/Sources.aspx?ID=54) pg. 76 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); telepathy 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +38, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +35, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +37, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +37, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +37, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +38 **Str** +6 **Dex** +9 **Con** +5 **Int** +6 **Wis** +10 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +36 **Will** +39 **HP** 350 , regeneration 30 (deactivated by lawful) **Immunities** acid, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) **Resistances** precision 20 **Weaknesses** lawful 20 **Unrelativity Field** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 1 mile. Creatures who start their turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 48 Will save or become unable to understand any communication (including [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37)), distinguish social rank or authority, or differentiate the relative value of objects or services. This persists for as long as the creature is in the aura. Creatures who succeed at the save are temporarily immune for 1 hour (24 hours on a critical success). [Proteans](/Traits.aspx?ID=231) are immune to the unrelativity field. The baatamidar can suppress this aura or activate it again as a free action. --- **Speed** fly 70 feet, swim 50 feet **Melee** tentacle +36 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+14 bludgeoning plus 2d6 chaotic plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 44 - **8th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (at will), [Mind Blank](/Spells.aspx?ID=192) (at will) - **9th** [Prismatic Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=232) - **10th** [Cataclysm](/Spells.aspx?ID=32) - **Constant (10th)** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (lawful only), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Mind-Numbing Grasp** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The baatamidar's tentacles leech away thought. A creature that starts its turn grabbed by the baatamidar must attempt a DC 41 Will save.
**Critical Success** Unaffected.
**Success** [Stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Failure** Stupefied 3 for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** Stupefied 3 for an unlimited duration. **Writhing Arms** The baatamidar makes up to four tentacle Strikes, each against a different target. Each attack counts separately for the baatamidar's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until the baatamidar has made all the attacks. If the baatamidar subsequently uses the [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) action, it can [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) any number of creatures it hit with Writhing Arms.
Making Allies of Enemies Baatamidar represent a tangible and imminent crisis to planar communities. When a baatamidar drifts into a region, locating and defeating it becomes a top priority for leaders of any alignment. Thus, celestial and infernal commanders alike make defeating baatamidars a priority, sometimes even joining together to exterminate the common threat.
name: Baatamidar

markdown:
Planar travel is at best imprecise, yet still too predictable for the erratic nature of the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19). Camaraches are spontaneous manifestations of the Maelstrom resisting the imposed order required to erect planar gates. Camaraches rarely leave the Maelstrom, but the internal skin of their rocky bodies constantly splits into temporary extraplanar portals and collapses again. The planar travel a camarach produces is wholly random, and even the camarach has no inkling where it sends victims. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Camarach](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1016) **Source** [Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege](/Sources.aspx?ID=54) pg. 77 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), thoughtsense (imprecise) 120 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); telepathy 100 feet, tongues **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +31 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +9 **Thoughtsense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The camarach senses the presence of non-mindless creatures within 120 feet as an imprecise sense. --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +26 **Will** +29 **HP** 320 **Resistances** physical 15 (except bludgeoning) **Weaknesses** lawful 15 --- **Speed** fly 60 feet, swim 60 feet **Melee** jaws +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+18 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 43 - **8th** [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144) (×3), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=193) (×3) - **9th** [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237) (at will; see project false image) - **Constant (7th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Disgorge Portal** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The camarach vomits a spray of coruscating light. This has the effect of a DC 42 [_prismatic spray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), except that each creature is affected by one violet beam and one other beam (roll 1d6 instead of 1d8 to determine which other beam). The camarach can't Disgorge a Portal again for 1d4 rounds. **Exile** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) The camarach shunts one creature within its body through an inner portal to send it away. The target must attempt a DC 38 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is teleported to an open space it chooses within 30 feet of the camarach.
**Success** The creature isn't teleported.
**Failure** The creature is teleported to a random open space 1 mile from the camarach.
**Critical Failure** The creature is teleported to a random other plane, as [_plane shift_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222). **Fast Swallow** **Trigger** The camarach [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) a creature; **Effect** The camarach uses [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35). **Project False Image** When the camarach casts [_project image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=237), its illusory image can be another creature of its size or a structure of its size that features a large portal (such as a freestanding archway or a large hut with an open doorway). **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** Huge, 3d10+9 bludgeoning, Rupture 34
A Serendipitous Journey A camarach has no control over where it shunts creatures; such precise control is antithetical to its nature. Yet these living portals can, if they choose, send creatures to where the creatures are most needed, or to the place that best advances their immediate goals (although the camarach never knows where that is). Camaraches don't generally do so, of course, but might if made friendly to heroes in need of planar travel.
name: Camarach

<title level="1" pfs="">[Sordesdaemon (Pollution Daemon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1017)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hulks of sewage and daemonic flesh, sordesdaemons embody death through pollution and are among the newest types of daemons to appear on Golarion. Sordesdaemons are fiendishly clever and often seek to inspire mortals with new ideas and inventions that despoil the environment.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 36

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Daemon_Sordesdaemon.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 15">[Sordesdaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1017)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Daemon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=213" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege](/Sources.aspx?ID=54) pg. 78

**Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); telepathy 100 feet

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +27, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +28

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +3

**Con** +9

**Int** +6

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 37 

**Fort** +30 

**Ref** +24 

**Will** +26 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 300

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)

**Weaknesses**
good 15

**Miasma of Pollution** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) 30 feet. Creatures in the aura can't reduce the value of the [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition. A creature that enters the aura or begins its turn in it must succeed at a DC 34 Fortitude save or be sickened 2 (plus [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) as long as it is sickened on a critical failure). A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute. Creatures made of water (such as [water elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=45)) and [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creatures use an outcome one degree of success worse than the result of their save.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +29 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning plus 1d6 evil and pollution infusion

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 38
- **1st**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; good only)
- **4th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will)
- **5th**
[Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42) (at will), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)
- **8th**
[Horrid Wilting](/Spells.aspx?ID=152), [Spiritual Epidemic](/Spells.aspx?ID=304)

**Pollution Infusion**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162)) Non-[fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) creatures adjacent to the afflicted creature take a –1 circumstance penalty to saving throws against disease; **Saving Throw** DC 36 Fortitude; **Stage 1** [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 day); **Stage 2** [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and drained 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** doomed 1 and drained 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** doomed 2 and drained 2 (1 week); **Stage 5** dead.

**Retch of Foulness** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The sordesdaemon exhales a spray of sewage that deals 8d6 acid damage and 8d6 poison damage in a 30-foot cone (DC 40 basic Fortitude save). It can't use Retch of Foulness again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Daemonic Pollution</title>

Sordesdaemons who aren't compelled to pursue a specific task often find their way into sewers beneath large cities, where they subjugate other creatures that wallow in filth, such as [ofalths](/Monsters.aspx?ID=316) and [otyughs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=327). They think nothing of sacrificing these minions if it advances their own aims.

</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-26" />
name: Sordesdaemon

<title level="1" pfs="">[Agradaemon (Conflagration Daemon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1018)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Equal parts lion, spider, and demagogue, agradaemons are born from the forge-flames of [Szuriel](/Deities.aspx?ID=136), Horseman of War. Although they're unwilling to work alongside each other for long, agradaemons frequently establish themselves as leaders of cults whose goal is to burn civilization to the ground. To these scheming monsters of flame and fear, torching a city is a mere diversion; incinerating a nation's entire agricultural system to turn its citizens into starving marauders before setting their forests and plains ablaze is truly satisfying.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 44

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Daemon_Agradaemon.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 19">[Agradaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1018)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Gargantuan" />
<trait label="Daemon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=213" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege](/Sources.aspx?ID=54) pg. 79

**Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), smoke vision

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +37, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +37, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +39

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +10

**Con** +5

**Int** +6

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +10
</row>

**Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair an agradaemon's vision; it ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 45 

**Fort** +31 

**Ref** +36 

**Will** +31 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 400

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)

**Weaknesses**
cold 20, good 20

**Choking Smog** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) 100 feet. The agradaemon is surrounded by ashy smoke. Creatures within the aura are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and creatures outside of it are concealed to creatures within it. Non-[daemon](/Traits.aspx?ID=213) creatures can't breathe in the aura and must hold their breath or [suffocate](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d12+14 slashing plus 2d6 fire and 2d6 evil

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
lava bomb +35 ([range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150)),
**Damage** 4d8 fire plus 2d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) plus 8 fire splash

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 41
- **1st**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; good only)
- **4th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will)
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)
- **8th**
[Volcanic Eruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=360) (×3)
- **10th**
[Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=186)

**Daemonic Pledge** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The agradaemon designates a single willing mortal as a pledged fanatic. That creature gains resistance to fire equal to its level, immunity to the agradaemon's choking smog aura, and smoke vision. The agradaemon can have up to 10 pledged fanatics at one time and can remove the status from any number of pledged fanatics as a free action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. The status can also be removed with an [_atone_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2) ritual.

**Proven Devotion** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The agradaemon regains 15 Hit Points, and a pledged fanatic of the agradaemon's choice that it can see loses 15 Hit Points (no effects apply that would decrease this Hit Point loss). A pledged creature that dies in this way explodes and deals 9d10 fire damage in a 20-foot emanation (DC 41 basic Reflex save).

**[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** <actions string="Single Action" />  claw

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Soul Fuel</title>

Whatever heat lay beneath the wastes of [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=12) bled away eons ago. Thereafter, the smiths forging weapons for [Szuriel](/Deities.aspx?ID=136), Horseman of War, turned to mortal souls as fuel for their fires. The flames of these unholy forges can generate profane creatures, and agradaemons are among the worst of these.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-26" />
name: Agradaemon

markdown:
Anchorites of the proteans, hegessiks live solitary lives roaming the dangerous wilds of the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19). There, they contemplate cosmic truths and dispense wisdom as itinerant mystics. Hegessiks proudly bear their ridges of golden feathers and gleaming gemstones, but it is within their third eye that their wisdom resides. Hegessiks normally have little interaction with the fluctuations of protean society and are more likely to be found liaising with non-protean monitors and followers of those deities—like [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12) and [Besmara](/Deities.aspx?ID=32)— who share the Maelstrom with proteans. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Hegessik](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1019) **Source** [Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege](/Sources.aspx?ID=54) pg. 80 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), entropy sense (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet, [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +29, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +29, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 **Entropy Sense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=132)) A hegessik can anticipate the most likely location of a creature through their supernatural insight into the forces of chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants the hegessik the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. The hegessik's entropy sense doesn't detect creatures under the effects of [_nondetection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=209) or that are otherwise shielded from [divinations](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) and [predictions](/Traits.aspx?ID=132). --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +23 **Will** +29 +1 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) magic **HP** 250 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 10) **Resistances** precision 10, protean anatomy 20 **Weaknesses** lawful 15 **Maddening Whispers** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. A susurrus of distracting whispers and dilations in reality surround a hegessik. When a non-[protean](/Traits.aspx?ID=231) ends its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 33 Will save. If it fails, it becomes confused for 1 round.
**Protean Anatomy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) A hegessik's vital organs shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the hegessik takes acid, electricity, or sonic damage, it gains the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the protean takes damage of one of the other types (in which case its resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first.
 The hegessik is immune to [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effects unless it is a willing target. If [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), the hegessik automatically recovers at the end of its next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet, swim 30 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +30 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+14 piercing plus 1d6 chaotic and warpwave strike **Melee** claw +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+14 slashing plus 1d6 chaotic **Melee** tail +30 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+14 bludgeoning plus 1d6 chaotic and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38 - **2nd** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; lawful only) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) - **6th** [Baleful Polymorph](/Spells.aspx?ID=17), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) (at will), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) (at will; self only) - **7th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (at will), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) - **8th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Divine Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=81) (chaotic only), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) (chaotic only) - **Constant (4th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The hegessik takes the appearance of any Large or smaller creature. This doesn't change its Speed or its attack and damage bonuses with its Strikes, but might change the damage type its Strikes deal. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d8+14 bludgeoning, DC 36 **Leaching Glare** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61)) The hegessik briefly opens its third eye. Non-protean creatures in a 30-foot cone must succeed at a DC 38 Will save or become [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round (stupefied 3 on a critical failure). **Telekinetic Reach** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) The hegessik manifests psychic versions of its natural attacks and makes a Strike with each of its jaws, claw, and tail, in any order. These Strikes have a reach of 60 feet. These attacks count toward the hegessik's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all the attacks. **Warpwave Strike** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Any creature struck and damaged by a hegessik's jaws Strike must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save or be subject to a warpwave (see Warpwave below).
Choruses of One Heggesiks are nominally included in the protean caste system, but they almost never join the protean choruses formed around shared philosophies. The hegessiks consider themselves to be outside such cliques, seeking their own enlightenment in the archetypal wildernesses of the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19).
name: Hegessik

markdown:
Occupying the pinnacle of the loose protean caste system, izfiitars enact the ever-shifting plans of the vaunted protean lords and those of the divine Speakers of the Depths. Izfiitars appear to be elevated versions of the protean priests known as [keketars](/Monsters.aspx?ID=338), despite their smaller size, although protean hierarchies are never so straightforward. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Izfiitar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1020) **Source** [Pathfinder #162: Ruins of the Radiant Siege](/Sources.aspx?ID=54) pg. 81 **Perception** +36; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), entropy sense (imprecise) 120 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet, [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +38, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +35, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +37, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +37, [Maelstrom Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +37, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +36, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +38, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +38 **Str** +9 **Dex** +10 **Con** +9 **Int** +7 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +9 **Entropy Sense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=132)) A izfiitar can anticipate the most likely location of a creature through their supernatural insight into the forces of chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants the izfiitar the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. The izfiitar's entropy sense doesn't detect creatures under the effects of [_nondetection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=209) or that are otherwise shielded from [divinations](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) and [predictions](/Traits.aspx?ID=132). --- **AC** 44 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +36 **Will** +38 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 360 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 20) **Resistances** acid 20, precision 20, protean anatomy 25 **Weaknesses** lawful 20 **Kiss of the Speakers** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The izfiitar continuously tinkers with the myriad possibilities in which it can move or manipulate magic. The izfiitar is always [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) and can use the extra action only to Cast a Spell, Step, or Stride.
**Prescient Revision** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Trigger** The izfiitar fails a check; **Effect** The izfiitar rerolls the triggering check and takes the better result. For 1d4 rounds, it loses the effects of Kiss of the Speakers and can't use Reshape Reality.
**Protean Anatomy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) A izfiitar's vital organs shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the izfiitar takes acid, electricity, or sonic damage, it gains the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the protean takes damage of one of the other types (in which case its resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first.
The izfiitar is immune to [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effects unless it is a willing target. If [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), the izfiitar automatically recovers at the end of its next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 50 feet, swim 40 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +38 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d8+17 piercing plus 2d6 chaotic and greater warpwave strike **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d6+17 slashing plus 2d6 chaotic and greater warpwave strike **Melee** tail +38 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+17 bludgeoning plus 2d6 chaotic and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 47 - **2nd** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; lawful only) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=54) (at will), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Hallucinatory Terrain](/Spells.aspx?ID=145) (at will; see Reshape Reality) - **6th** [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) (at will; self only) - **7th** [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) (at will) - **8th** [Baleful Polymorph](/Spells.aspx?ID=17), [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48) (at will), [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (at will), [Prismatic Wall](/Spells.aspx?ID=234) - **9th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) (chaotic only), [Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=186), [Overwhelming Presence](/Spells.aspx?ID=212), [Prismatic Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=232) - **10th** [Miracle](/Spells.aspx?ID=196) - **Constant (4th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The izfiitar takes the appearance of any Huge or smaller creature. This doesn't change its Speed or its attack and damage bonuses with its Strikes, but might change the damage type its Strikes deal. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d8+17 bludgeoning, DC 44 **Greater Warpwave Strike** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Any creature struck and damaged by an izfiitar's jaws or claw Strike must succeed at a DC 42 Fortitude save or be subject to a particularly powerful warpwave (see Warpwave below). Roll twice and apply both affects, rerolling any duplicates. **Reshape Reality** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When the izfiitar casts [_hallucinatory terrain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=145), it infuses the illusion with quasi-real substance. Creatures that do not disbelieve the illusion treat structures and terrain created through the spell as though they were real, ascending illusory stairs, becoming trapped by illusory quicksand, and so on. **Storm of Claws** The izfiitar makes up to six claw Strikes, each against a different target.
Heralds of the Speakers Izfiitars with the greatest authority have even greater powers, such as the ability to cleave off portions of other planes into the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19) or to flaunt the laws of reality to redirect spell effects at their whims.
name: Izfiitar
