**Fort** +20 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +16 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 140

</row>

**Immunities**
acid

**Resistances**
bludgeoning 10

**Powerful Stench** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) 20 feet. A creature that enters the aura must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34); on a critical failure, the creature is also [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for as long as it is sickened. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +20 ([deadly 1d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d10+10 piercing plus 1d4 [persistent acid](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
spit +17 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150)),
**Damage** 2d6 acid plus 1d6 [persistent acid](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and 1d6 acid [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150)

**Bouncing Crush** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The gibtanius [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) and can end this Leap occupying the same space as other creatures. It deals 5d6 bludgeoning damage to each creature in the space it Leaps to (DC 26 basic Reflex save), then [Shoves](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) each of those creatures. If it fails to Shove any creature out of its space, it bounces to the closest available space of its choosing that does not contain any creatures. Bouncing Crush counts as one attack when determining the gibtanius's multiple attack penalty.

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The gibtanius vomits a stream of acid that deals 9d6 acid damage to all creatures in a 60-foot line (DC 26 basic Reflex save). It can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-213" />

The most common seugathis spawned by [neothelids](/Monsters.aspx?ID=739) are seugathi servants. Their masters equip them with tools useful in their tasks (often a wand and a weapon), and they rarely value other material things beyond their usefulness in completing their imprinted mission. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Seugathi Servant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1048) **Source** [Pathfinder #164: Hands of the Devil](/Sources.aspx?ID=75) pg. 82 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +10 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Items** [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31) --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +17 **Will** +12 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 75 **Immunities** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) **Resistances** bludgeoning 5 **Mindfog Aura** ([enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 20 feet. A creature that starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 21 Will save or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round; on a success, that creature is temporarily immune for 1 minute. A seugathi can suppress or activate this aura as a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
**Command Confusion** **Trigger** A creature fails its save against the seugathi's mindfog aura; **Effect** The seugathi determines who the confused creature attacks for that round, instead of the target being randomly determined by the GM. If the chosen target is the confused creature's ally, the creature can immediately attempt a DC 21 Will save; on a success, its target is determined randomly as normal for confusion, and on a critical success the target is no longer confused. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** bite +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+5 piercing plus seugathi venom **Melee** longsword +14 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d8+5 piercing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 24, attack +16 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **3rd** [Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=156), [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=170), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) (×3) **Envenom Weapon** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The seugathi applies their seugathi venom to one weapon they wield. **Magic Item Mastery** A seugathi can Cast a Spell from a magic item even if the spell isn't on their spell list. All such spells are occult spells and use the seugathi's innate spell DC and attack modifier. **Seugathi Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 21 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) and stupefied 2 (1 round)

markdown:
Seugathi reality warpers create and destroy inhospitable environments on a small scale by manipulating the forces of raw chaos. A [neothelid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=739) might spawn such a seugathi to create habitats for unique slaves or guests, or to destroy the ecosystem of a troublesome species. Darklands scholars refer to reality warpers as veterans in the seugathi ranks, but they're more accurately compared to aggressive landscape architects. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Seugathi Reality Warper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1049) **Source** [Pathfinder #164: Hands of the Devil](/Sources.aspx?ID=75) pg. 83 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +19, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +18, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +5 **Items** [_+1 battle axe_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +21 **Will** +15 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 120 **Immunities** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), poison **Resistances** bludgeoning 10 **Mindfog Aura** ([enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 20 feet. A creature that starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 25 Will save or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round; on a success, that creature is temporarily immune for 1 minute. A seugathi can suppress or activate this aura as a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
**Command Confusion** **Trigger** A creature fails its save against the seugathi's mindfog aura; **Effect** The seugathi determines who the confused creature attacks for that round, instead of the target being randomly determined by the GM. If the chosen target is the confused creature's ally, the creature can immediately attempt a DC 25 Will save; on a success, its target is determined randomly as normal for confusion, and on a critical success the target is no longer confused --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** bite +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+8 piercing plus 1d10 chaotic plus seugathi venom **Melee** battle axe +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d8+8 bludgeoning plus 1d10 chaotic **Occult Innate Spells** DC 28, attack +20 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **3rd** [Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=156) (×3), [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=170) (×3), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) (×3) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48) (×3), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **5th** [Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23), [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57), [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144), [Synesthesia](/Spells.aspx?ID=328) **Envenom Weapon** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The seugathi applies their seugathi venom to one weapon they wield. **Magic Item Mastery** A seugathi can Cast a Spell from a magic item even if the spell isn't on their spell list. All such spells are occult spells and use the seugathi's innate spell DC and attack modifier. **Seugathi Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) and stupefied 2 (1 round) **Warp Reality** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The seugathi bends reality in their choice of a 10-foot emanation or a 60-foot line, altering the terrain of the area. The terrain becomes a different type of terrain (such as aquatic, arctic, or desert) and becomes normal or difficult terrain, as the seugathi chooses. Structures, general geographic features, and creatures in the area aren't transformed, but creatures in the area take 5d6 chaotic damage (DC 28 basic Fortitude save) as reality tries to bend them along with the terrain. Though changes to the area are permanent, the natural environment might eventually revert the land back to its original state (aquatic areas drain, arctic areas thaw, and so on).
name: Seugathi Reality Warper

<title level="1" pfs="">[Shanrigol Heap](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1050)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The most basic and common type of shanrigol is called, based on its general shape, a shanrigol heap. These amalgamations of warped flesh and shattered bone establish hunting grounds by accident rather than design, remaining where prey has been plentiful in the past so they can grow larger and larger as they add to their jumbled forms. Only rare fleshwarpers create these abominations willingly, as they ignore all commands and containment to seek out prey.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 21

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 21

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Shanrigol_ShanrigolHeap.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Shanrigol Heap](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1050)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #164: Hands of the Devil](/Sources.aspx?ID=75) pg. 84

**Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +2

**Con** +3

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** -5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1)

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +9 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 55
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
slashing 5, piercing 5

**Weaknesses**
[positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fleshy slap +14 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180)),
**Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bone shard +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d8+8 bludgeoning

**Sapping Squeeze** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The shanrigol has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The shanrigol squeezes the life essence from its target, dealing 1d6+5 bludgeoning and 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (DC 18 basic Fortitude save). The shanrigol regains temporary Hit Points equal to half the amount of bludgeoning damage a single target takes; these temporary Hit Points last for 1 minute.

**Shred Flesh**   A shanrigol rips flesh away from targets in great chunks. Creatures critically hit by the shanrigol's fleshy slap Strike take an additional 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and are [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) for as long as they are bleeding.

**Undulating Step** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The shanrigol separates its flesh from its bones to fit through small spaces. It Strides, ignoring difficult terrain and fitting through tight spaces as though it were a Tiny creature. It can't use any actions other than Undulating Step until it occupies an area large enough for its normal form.

</column>
